5655	  \label{lem:dual_restrict_iso}
5656	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.dual_restrict_iso}
5657	  \uses{lem:internal_hom_isSheaf, lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_dualequiv}
5658	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], "Modules on Ringed Spaces", \S Internal Hom,
5659	  % Lemma~\texttt{lemma-pullback-internal-hom} (tag area 01CM)
5660	  % (read from references/stacks-modules.tex, L3710--L3721).
5661	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
5662	  % "Let $f : (X, \mathcal{O}_X) \to (Y, \mathcal{O}_Y)$ be a morphism
5663	  % of ringed spaces. Let $\mathcal{F}$, $\mathcal{G}$ be $\mathcal{O}_Y$-modules.
5664	  % If $\mathcal{F}$ is finitely presented and $f$ is flat,
5665	  % then the canonical map
5666	  % $$
5667	  % f^*\SheafHom_{\mathcal{O}_Y}(\mathcal{F}, \mathcal{G})
5668	  % \longrightarrow
5669	  % \SheafHom_{\mathcal{O}_X}(f^*\mathcal{F}, f^*\mathcal{G})
5670	  % $$
5671	  % is an isomorphism."
5672	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], ``Modules on Ringed Spaces'', \S Internal Hom,
5673	  Lemma~\texttt{lemma-pullback-internal-hom} (tag area 01CM).}
5674	  Let \(f : Y \hookrightarrow X\) be an open immersion of schemes, with image the open
5675	  \(U \subseteq X\), and let \(M \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\). Then there is a
5676	  canonical isomorphism of \(\mathcal{O}_Y\)-modules
5677	  \[
5678	    (\mathtt{dual}\,M)\big|_f \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\; \mathtt{dual}\bigl(M|_f\bigr),
5679	  \]
5680	  natural in \(M\), between the restriction of the sheaf-level dual
5681	  (\cref{lem:internal_hom_isSheaf}) and the dual of the restriction. (Only opens
5682	  \(V \subseteq U\) intervene downstream, where the comparison is needed.)
5683	\end{lemma}
5684	
5685	\begin{proof}
5686	  \uses{lem:internal_hom_isSheaf, lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_dualequiv}
5687	  This is the classical internal-hom base-change isomorphism
5688	  \(f^*\mathcal{H}\!om_{\mathcal{O}_X}(M, \mathcal{O}_X) \cong
5689	  \mathcal{H}\!om_{\mathcal{O}_Y}(f^*M, f^*\mathcal{O}_X)\) of
5690	  Lemma~\texttt{lemma-pullback-internal-hom}, specialised to the structure sheaf
5691	  \(\mathcal{G} = \mathcal{O}_X\). For an open immersion the morphism \(f\) is flat and
5692	  restriction \((-)|_f\) is the exact pullback \(f^*\), so the finite-presentation and
5693	  flatness hypotheses of the general ringed-space statement are automatic; this is the
5694	  \emph{favorable} (open-immersion) case of the base-change comparison, in which the map
5695	  is an isomorphism for \emph{every} \(M\), not only the finitely-presented ones.
5696	
5697	  We emphasise at the outset that this is a genuine \emph{natural isomorphism}, not a
5698	  definitional equality, and --- crucially --- that the dual is \emph{not sectionwise}.
5699	  Unlike the tensor product, whose value is computed fibrewise
5700	  (\((M \otimes N)(V) = M(V) \otimes N(V)\)), the dual evaluates to a Hom over an entire
5701	  down-set: for an open \(V \subseteq U\),
5702	  \[
5703	    (\mathtt{dual}\,M)(V)
5704	      \;=\; \mathcal{H}om\bigl(M|_V,\ \mathcal{O}|_V\bigr)\big|_{\mathtt{Over}\,V},
5705	  \]
5706	  a module of morphisms of presheaves over the whole slice \(\mathtt{Over}\,V \subseteq
5707	  \mathrm{Opens}\,Y\), \emph{not} a single fibre \(M(V) \to \mathcal{O}(V)\). Consequently
5708	  the assertion that restriction along the open immersion \(f\) commutes with the dual does
5709	  \emph{not} reduce to a single ground-ring reconciliation. The proof proceeds in two
5710	  stages: a preliminary adjunction-uniqueness rewrite (stage~H1) that re-presents the
5711	  pulled-back dual in pushforward form, followed by the resolution of the resulting
5712	  \emph{residual} into \emph{two} legs, carried out objectwise in the open \(V\) (with
5713	  \(V \subseteq U\)) and assembled over the slice with poset-thin naturality.
5714	
5715	  \emph{Stage H1 --- adjunction-uniqueness rewrite.} The pulled-back dual is initially
5716	  presented in the \(\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi\)-form inherited from the abstract
5717	  definition of the restriction functor, and no sectionwise comparison can begin against
5718	  that opaque form. We first rewrite it into a \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta\)-form: the
5719	  restriction-along-an-open-immersion pullback and the pushforward along the structure-ring
5720	  map \(\beta\) are both left adjoints to one and the same functor, so by uniqueness of left
5721	  adjoints --- the adjunction \(\mathtt{pushforwardPushforwardAdj}\) composed with
5722	  \(\mathtt{leftAdjointUniq}\) --- they are canonically isomorphic, and this isomorphism
5723	  transports the pulled-back dual into pushforward form. After H1 the residual obligation is
5724	  \emph{exactly}
5725	  \[
5726	    (\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta).\mathtt{obj}\,(\mathtt{dual}\,M.\mathtt{val})
5727	      \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
5728	    \mathtt{dual}\bigl((\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta).\mathtt{obj}\,M.\mathtt{val}\bigr),
5729	  \]
5730	  i.e.\ ``\(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta\) commutes with the dual''. It is this residual ---
5731	  \emph{not} the original Step-4 isomorphism --- that legs (A) and (B) below resolve; legs
5732	  (A) and (B) are the content remaining \emph{after} the H1 rewrite, not a standalone
5733	  two-leg split of the whole step.
5734	
5735	  \emph{Leg (A) --- slice-site Hom base-change (Beck--Chevalley).} The domain presheaf must
5736	  first be transported across the open immersion. Because \(f\) is an open immersion, its
5737	  direct-image functor on open sets is fully faithful with image
5738	  the down-set \(\{W : W \le U\}\); this carries the restricted domain
5739	  \(\mathtt{restr}_V(f_*M)\) over \(\mathtt{Over}\,V \subseteq \mathrm{Opens}\,Y\) to the
5740	  restricted domain \(\mathtt{restr}_{fV}\,M\) over \(\mathtt{Over}\,(fV) \subseteq
5741	  \mathrm{Opens}\,X\), and hence identifies the two Hom-modules computed over these matched
5742	  slices. This is a genuine slice-site Hom base-change --- a Beck--Chevalley square on the
5743	  internal hom across the fully-faithful open-immersion functor, restricted to the down-set
5744	  of \(fV\) --- \emph{not} a fibrewise identity.
5745	
5746	  \emph{Leg (B) --- ground-ring reconciliation.} The codomain of the internal hom is in
5747	  both cases the structure sheaf. Once leg (A) has matched the domains, the unit codomain
5748	  is reconciled along the open-immersion ring isomorphism
5749	  \(\beta_V = (\mathtt{toRingCatSheafHom}\,f).\mathrm{hom}_V \colon
5750	  \mathcal{O}_X(fV) \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{O}_Y(V)\): restriction of scalars along the
5751	  ring \emph{isomorphism} \(\beta_V\) is an equivalence, and its ModuleCat-level
5752	  shadow on duals is exactly \cref{lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_dualequiv}, carrying the
5753	  \(\mathcal{O}_X(fV)\)-linear dual onto the \(\mathcal{O}_Y(V)\)-linear dual,
5754	  \(R(V)\)-linearly and invertibly. This is the dual analogue, at the module level, of the
5755	  ring-isomorphism tensor equivalence (\cref{lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_strongmonoidal}).
5756	
5757	  \emph{The leg-(A) atom \(\mathtt{sliceDualTransport}\).} Leg (A) is realised by a single
5758	  \(\mathcal{O}_Y(V)\)-linear equivalence \(\mathtt{sliceDualTransport}\,f\,M\,V\), built
5759	  \emph{by hand} --- it is \emph{not} obtained by consuming a sheaf-level slice equivalence.
5760	  This is essential: the equivalence of \emph{sheaves of modules} over an open immersion
5761	  compares the restriction functors and the units, but carries \emph{no} internal-hom or
5762	  dual content; the assertion realized by leg (A) --- that the dual commutes with slice
5763	  reindexing along the open-immersion direct-image functor \(f_{!}\) on opens
5764	  (\(f.\mathtt{opensFunctor}\)) --- cannot be supplied by such an object without a
5765	  monoidal-closed structure on sheaves of modules, which this development deliberately does
5766	  \emph{not} carry (cf.\ \cref{rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path}). Leg (A) must
5767	  therefore be constructed directly.
5768	
5769	  Concretely, \(\mathtt{sliceDualTransport}\,f\,M\,V\) is the \(\mathtt{eqToHom}\)-conjugation
5770	  of the slice-Hom components across the open-immersion functor \(f.\mathtt{opensFunctor}\),
5771	  transported along the down-set identity \(\mathtt{image\_preimage\_of\_le}\) --- the image
5772	  under the open immersion of the preimage of an open \(V \subseteq U\) is \(V\) again,
5773	  \(\iota\bigl(\iota^{-1}(V)\bigr) = V\). Because \(\mathrm{Opens}\,Y\) is a thin poset, the
5774	  naturality squares for this conjugation commute by \(\mathtt{Subsingleton.elim}\); this is
5775	  exactly the pattern already established for \(\mathtt{homLocalSection}\) and
5776	  \(\mathtt{dualUnitIsoGen}\). The forward and inverse maps are this conjugation and its
5777	  reverse, identifying the slice-Hom value over \(\mathtt{Over}\,(fV)\) with the slice-Hom
5778	  value over \(\mathtt{Over}\,V\), \(\mathcal{O}_Y(V)\)-linearly.
5779	
5780	  The Step-4 residual is then \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.isoMk}\) of the sectionwise
5781	  composite of (leg (A), \(\mathtt{sliceDualTransport}\)) followed by (leg (B),
5782	  \(\mathtt{restrictScalarsRingIsoDualEquiv}\) along the open-immersion ring isomorphism
5783	  \((f.\mathtt{appIso}\,V).\mathrm{inv}\), \cref{lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_dualequiv}),
5784	  with the outer naturality thin-poset-trivial.
5785	
5786	  The residual of stage~H1 (``\(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta\) commutes with the dual'') is
5787	  the sectionwise composite of leg (A) followed by leg (B), packaged over the slice with
5788	  poset-thin naturality; the full Step-4 isomorphism is then the H1 isomorphism followed by
5789	  this composite. The slice-reindexing coherence is \emph{light}, because \(\mathrm{Opens}\)
5790	  is a thin poset and the relevant squares commute by \(\mathtt{Subsingleton.elim}\),
5791	  whereas H1 and legs (A) and (B) carry the substantive content.
5792	
5793	  It is worth recording \emph{why} the strong-monoidal restriction
5794	  (\cref{lem:presheaf_pushforward_adj_substrate}) has no direct dual analogue here. For
5795	  the tensor product the transport \emph{collapsed to leg (B) alone}: because \(\otimes\)
5796	  is sectionwise, pushing a tensor forward reduced, section by section, to a single
5797	  ground-ring tensor equivalence, with no domain slice left to re-index. The dual's
5798	  non-sectionwise nature removes that collapse and forces the additional leg (A): the
5799	  domain \(\mathtt{restr}_V(f_*M)\) genuinely lives over a varying slice and must be
5800	  transported before the ground ring can be reconciled.
5801	
5802	  We caution that the naive ``fixed-value-category slice equivalence'' of
5803	  \cref{lem:open_immersion_slice_sheaf_equiv} is \emph{not} applicable to leg (A). That instrument
5804	  operates in a fixed value category over a fixed base ring, whereas the residual of
5805	  leg (A) lives at the \emph{presheaf} level, with a per-section ring \(\mathcal{O}_Y(V)\)
5806	  that varies with \(V\) and a finer slicing than the sheaf-level equivalence resolves;
5807	  leg (A) must therefore be built directly at the presheaf-of-modules / varying-ring level.
5808	
5809	  Composing legs (A) and (B) gives, for each \(V \subseteq U\), an
5810	  \(\mathcal{O}_Y(V)\)-linear isomorphism between the two values; assembling these into a
5811	  presheaf-of-modules natural isomorphism and sheafifying through the sheaf-level dual of
5812	  \cref{lem:internal_hom_isSheaf} resolves the H1 residual. Precomposing with the stage-H1
5813	  rewrite then yields the stated isomorphism
5814	  \((\mathtt{dual}\,M)|_f \cong \mathtt{dual}(M|_f)\).

628	noncomputable def homModule (M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
629	    Module (R.obj (Opposite.op T) : Type u) (M ⟶ N) where
630	  smul f φ := φ ≫ globalSMul hT N f
631	  one_smul φ := by change φ ≫ globalSMul hT N 1 = φ; rw [globalSMul_one, Category.comp_id]
632	  mul_smul f g φ := by
633	    change φ ≫ globalSMul hT N (f * g) = (φ ≫ globalSMul hT N g) ≫ globalSMul hT N f
634	    rw [globalSMul_mul, Category.assoc]
635	  smul_zero f := by change (0 : M ⟶ N) ≫ globalSMul hT N f = 0; rw [Limits.zero_comp]
636	  zero_smul φ := by change φ ≫ globalSMul hT N 0 = 0; rw [globalSMul_zero, Limits.comp_zero]
637	  smul_add f φ ψ := by
638	    change (φ + ψ) ≫ globalSMul hT N f = φ ≫ globalSMul hT N f + ψ ≫ globalSMul hT N f
639	    rw [Preadditive.add_comp]
640	  add_smul f g φ := by
641	    change φ ≫ globalSMul hT N (f + g) = φ ≫ globalSMul hT N f + φ ≫ globalSMul hT N g
642	    rw [globalSMul_add, Preadditive.comp_add]
643	
644	/-! ### The slice value at an object `U` via the over-category `Over U`
645	
646	The slice internal hom `ℋom(M, N)(U) := ModuleCat.of (R(U)) (M|_U ⟶ N|_U)` of
647	blueprint `def:presheaf_internal_hom` is realized object-by-object here. The
648	restriction `M|_U` is `PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀` along the over-category
649	projection `Over.forget U : Over U ⥤ C`; the over-category has terminal object
650	`Over.mk (𝟙 U)` (`Over.mkIdTerminal`), at which the restricted ring is `R(U)`
651	(by `rfl`), so the `R(U)`-module structure on `M|_U ⟶ N|_U` is exactly `homModule`
652	applied to that terminal. -/
653	
654	/-- **Restriction of a presheaf of modules to the over-category `Over U`.** This is
655	the `M|_U` of the slice internal-hom formula: `PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀` along
656	the over-category projection `Over.forget U`. The new base presheaf of rings is
657	`(Over.forget U).op ⋙ R`, whose value at the terminal `Over.mk (𝟙 U)` is `R(U)`. -/
658	noncomputable def restr (U : C)
659	    (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
660	    PresheafOfModules.{u} (((Over.forget U).op ⋙ R) ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :=
661	  (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀ (Over.forget U) (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)).obj M
662	
663	/-- **The slice internal-hom value at `U`: `Hom(M|_U, N|_U)` as an `R(U)`-module.**
664	This is the `R(U)`-module underlying `ℋom(M, N)(U)` of blueprint
665	`def:presheaf_internal_hom`: the morphism group of the over-category restrictions
666	`M|_U ⟶ N|_U`, equipped with the `homModule` action for the terminal object
667	`Over.mk (𝟙 U)` of `Over U`. The full presheaf `internalHom` (the assembly of these
668	values over the restriction maps `V ⟶ U`) was assembled iter-220 (`internalHom`). -/
669	@[implicit_reducible]
670	noncomputable def internalHomObjModule (U : C)
671	    (M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
672	    Module (R.obj (Opposite.op U) : Type u) (restr U M ⟶ restr U N) :=
673	  homModule (R := (Over.forget U).op ⋙ R) (T := Over.mk (𝟙 U)) Over.mkIdTerminal
674	    (restr U M) (restr U N)
675	
676	/-- **The restriction map of the presheaf internal hom.** For a morphism `g : V ⟶ U`
677	of `C`, restricting a morphism `φ : M|_U ⟶ N|_U` of restricted presheaves of modules
678	along `Over.map g : Over V ⥤ Over U` yields a morphism `M|_V ⟶ N|_V`. Realised as
679	`(pushforward₀ (Over.map g) …).map φ`; the target base ring presheaf
680	`(Over.map g).op ⋙ (Over.forget U).op ⋙ R` is definitionally `(Over.forget V).op ⋙ R`,
681	so the result has the expected type `restr V M ⟶ restr V N`. This is the
682	further-restriction map `φ ↦ φ|_V` of blueprint `lem:presheaf_internal_hom_restriction`. -/
683	noncomputable def restrictionMap {U V : C} (g : V ⟶ U)
684	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
685	    (φ : restr U M ⟶ restr U N) : restr V M ⟶ restr V N :=
686	  (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀ (Over.map g)
687	    ((Over.forget U).op ⋙ (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))).map φ
688	
689	/-- **`restrictionMap` is additive.** Part of the additivity assertion of blueprint
690	`lem:presheaf_internal_hom_restriction`. -/
691	lemma restrictionMap_add {U V : C} (g : V ⟶ U)
692	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
693	    (φ ψ : restr U M ⟶ restr U N) :
694	    restrictionMap g (φ + ψ) = restrictionMap g φ + restrictionMap g ψ := by
695	  ext1 X; rfl
696	
697	/-- **`restrictionMap` preserves zero.** -/
698	lemma restrictionMap_zero {U V : C} (g : V ⟶ U)
699	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)} :
700	    restrictionMap g (0 : restr U M ⟶ restr U N) = 0 := by
701	  ext1 X; rfl
702	
703	/-- Helper: the component of a morphism of presheaves of modules at two *equal*
704	objects agrees up to `HEq`. Proven by `subst`. Used to discharge the
705	pseudofunctoriality coherence of `restrictionMap` (`Over.map` is only functorial in
706	its argument up to `Over.mapId_eq` / `Over.mapComp_eq`). -/
707	private lemma hom_app_heq {B : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} B}
708	    (φ : M ⟶ N) {X Y : Cᵒᵖ} (h : X = Y) : HEq (φ.app X) (φ.app Y) := by
709	  subst h; rfl
710	
711	/-- **Functoriality of `restrictionMap`: identity.** -/
712	lemma restrictionMap_id {U : C}
713	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
714	    (φ : restr U M ⟶ restr U N) :
715	    restrictionMap (𝟙 U) φ = φ := by
716	  ext1 X
717	  exact eq_of_heq (hom_app_heq φ (by rw [Over.mapId_eq]; rfl))
718	
719	/-- **Functoriality of `restrictionMap`: composition.** For `g : V ⟶ U`, `h : W ⟶ V`,
720	restricting along `h ≫ g` is restricting along `g` then along `h`. -/
721	lemma restrictionMap_comp {U V W : C} (g : V ⟶ U) (h : W ⟶ V)
722	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
723	    (φ : restr U M ⟶ restr U N) :
724	    restrictionMap (h ≫ g) φ = restrictionMap h (restrictionMap g φ) := by
725	  ext1 X
726	  exact eq_of_heq (hom_app_heq φ (by rw [Over.mapComp_eq]; rfl))
727	
728	/-- **`restrictionMap` respects composition of morphisms** (functoriality of
729	`pushforward₀.map`). -/
730	lemma restrictionMap_comp_hom {U V : C} (g : V ⟶ U)
731	    {M N P : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
732	    (φ : restr U M ⟶ restr U N) (ψ : restr U N ⟶ restr U P) :
733	    restrictionMap g (φ ≫ ψ) = restrictionMap g φ ≫ restrictionMap g ψ :=
734	  Functor.map_comp _ _ _
735	
736	/-- **Restriction commutes with the global-scalar endomorphism.** For `g : V ⟶ U`
737	and a global scalar `r ∈ R(U)`, restricting the multiplication-by-`r` endomorphism of
738	`N|_U` yields the multiplication-by-`R(g)(r)` endomorphism of `N|_V`. This is the heart
739	of the semilinearity of `restrictionMap` (blueprint `lem:presheaf_internal_hom_restriction`):
740	both sides act on each section by scalar multiplication, and the scalars agree by
741	functoriality of `R` (`termRingMap_naturality`). -/
742	lemma restrictionMap_globalSMul {U V : C} (g : V ⟶ U)
743	    {N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
744	    (r : (R.obj (Opposite.op U) : Type u)) :
745	    restrictionMap g (globalSMul (R := (Over.forget U).op ⋙ R) (T := Over.mk (𝟙 U))
746	        Over.mkIdTerminal (restr U N) r)
747	      = globalSMul (R := (Over.forget V).op ⋙ R) (T := Over.mk (𝟙 V))
748	        Over.mkIdTerminal (restr V N) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (R.map g.op)) r) := by
749	  ext1 Y
750	  ext m
751	  rw [globalSMul_hom_apply]
752	  erw [globalSMul_hom_apply]
753	  congr 1
754	  simp only [termRingMap, Functor.comp_map]
755	  have hmor : (Over.forget U).op.map
756	        (Over.mkIdTerminal.from ((Over.map g).op.obj Y).unop).op
757	      = g.op ≫ (Over.forget V).op.map (Over.mkIdTerminal.from Y.unop).op := by
758	    apply Quiver.Hom.unop_inj
759	    change Over.Hom.left (Over.mkIdTerminal.from ((Over.map g).obj Y.unop))
760	      = Over.Hom.left (Over.mkIdTerminal.from Y.unop) ≫ g
761	    have e1 : Over.Hom.left (Over.mkIdTerminal.from ((Over.map g).obj Y.unop))
762	        = ((Over.map g).obj Y.unop).hom := by simp
763	    have e2 : Over.Hom.left (Over.mkIdTerminal.from Y.unop) = Y.unop.hom := by simp
764	    rw [e1, e2]
765	    simp [Over.map_obj_hom]
766	  rw [hmor]
767	  erw [← CommRingCat.comp_apply, ← R.map_comp]
768	  rfl
769	
770	/-- **`restrictionMap` packaged as an additive group homomorphism** on the morphism
771	groups, using `restrictionMap_zero` and `restrictionMap_add`. This is the value of the
772	underlying `Ab`-presheaf morphism of `internalHom`. -/
773	noncomputable def restrictionMapAddHom {U V : C} (g : V ⟶ U)
774	    (M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
775	    (restr U M ⟶ restr U N) →+ (restr V M ⟶ restr V N) where
776	  toFun := restrictionMap g
777	  map_zero' := restrictionMap_zero g
778	  map_add' := restrictionMap_add g
779	
780	/-- **The underlying `Ab`-valued presheaf of `internalHom M N`**: `U ↦ (M|_U ⟶ N|_U)`
781	with the further-restriction maps. Functoriality is `restrictionMap_id` /
782	`restrictionMap_comp`. This is piece (a)+(b)+(c) of blueprint `def:presheaf_internal_hom`
783	at the abelian-group level; the `R(U)`-module refinement is added by `internalHom`. -/
784	noncomputable def internalHomPresheaf
785	    (M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
786	    Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat.{max u uC vC} where
787	  obj X := AddCommGrpCat.of (restr X.unop M ⟶ restr X.unop N)
788	  map {X Y} f := AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (restrictionMapAddHom f.unop M N)
789	  map_id X := by
790	    apply AddCommGrpCat.hom_ext
791	    refine AddMonoidHom.ext fun φ => ?_
792	    exact restrictionMap_id φ
793	  map_comp {X Y Z} f f' := by
794	    apply AddCommGrpCat.hom_ext
795	    refine AddMonoidHom.ext fun φ => ?_
796	    exact restrictionMap_comp f.unop f'.unop φ
797	
798	/-- **Semilinearity of `restrictionMap`** in the form consumed by
799	`PresheafOfModules.ofPresheaf`: for `f : X ⟶ Y` in `Cᵒᵖ`, a global scalar
800	`r ∈ R(X)` and `m : M|_{X} ⟶ N|_{X}`, `restrictionMap f.unop (r • m) =
801	R(f)(r) • restrictionMap f.unop m`, where the `•`'s are the slice-value module
802	actions (`internalHomObjModule`). This is `lem:presheaf_internal_hom_restriction`'s
803	compatibility datum; it follows from `restrictionMap_comp_hom` and
804	`restrictionMap_globalSMul`. -/
805	lemma restrictionMap_smul {X Y : Cᵒᵖ} (f : X ⟶ Y)
806	    (M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))
807	    (r : (R.obj X : Type u)) (m : restr X.unop M ⟶ restr X.unop N) :
808	    restrictionMap f.unop
809	        (letI := internalHomObjModule X.unop M N; r • m)
810	      = (letI := internalHomObjModule Y.unop M N;
811	          (ConcreteCategory.hom (R.map f)) r • restrictionMap f.unop m) := by
812	  change restrictionMap f.unop (m ≫ globalSMul Over.mkIdTerminal (restr X.unop N) r)
813	    = restrictionMap f.unop m
814	        ≫ globalSMul Over.mkIdTerminal (restr Y.unop N) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (R.map f)) r)
815	  rw [restrictionMap_comp_hom, restrictionMap_globalSMul, Quiver.Hom.op_unop]
816	
817	end InternalHom
818	
819	/-! ### Assembly of the presheaf internal hom over a single-universe base
820	
821	`PresheafOfModules.ofPresheaf` ties the underlying `Ab`-presheaf to the ground ring
822	presheaf `R`'s universe (`Type u`), but for a general base category the morphism groups
823	`M|_U ⟶ N|_U` live in `Type (max u uC vC)`. The two coincide exactly when the base
824	category is single-universe (`Category.{u, u}`), which is the case for the topological
825	site `Opens X` underlying the structure sheaf of a scheme. The full presheaf internal
826	hom is therefore assembled in this specialised universe context. -/
827	
828	namespace InternalHom
829	
830	section Assembly
831	
832	variable {D : Type u} [Category.{u, u} D] {R₀ : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
833	
834	/-- **The presheaf internal hom `ℋom(M, N)`** (blueprint `def:presheaf_internal_hom`):
835	the presheaf of `R`-modules assembled from the slice value modules
836	`internalHomObjModule` (a), the further-restriction maps `internalHomPresheaf` (b)(c),
837	and the semilinearity datum `restrictionMap_smul`, via `PresheafOfModules.ofPresheaf`.
838	Stated over a single-universe base (e.g. the topological site `Opens X`); see the
839	section note for the universe constraint. -/
840	noncomputable def internalHom
841	    (M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
842	    PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :=
843	  @PresheafOfModules.ofPresheaf D _ (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)
844	    (internalHomPresheaf M N)
845	    (fun X => internalHomObjModule X.unop M N)
846	    (fun {_ _} f r m => restrictionMap_smul f M N r m)
847	
848	end Assembly
849	
850	end InternalHom
851	
852	namespace InternalHom
853	
854	open CategoryTheory Limits Opposite
855	
856	universe vC uC
857	
858	variable {C : Type uC} [Category.{vC} C] {R : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
859	  {T : C} (hT : IsTerminal T)
860	
861	/-- **`termRingMap` at the terminal object itself is the identity.** The unique
862	`T → T` is the identity, so the induced ring map `R(T) → R(T)` is `id`. This is the
863	fact that the global-scalar action evaluated at the terminal section is the scalar
864	itself, used to prove the evaluation map of `internalHomEval` is `R(U)`-bilinear. -/
865	lemma termRingMap_terminal (f : (R.obj (op T) : Type u)) :
866	    (ConcreteCategory.hom (termRingMap hT (op T))) f = f := by
867	  have hid : hT.from T = 𝟙 T := hT.hom_ext _ _
868	  simp only [termRingMap, unop_op, hid, op_id, R.map_id]
869	  rfl
870	
871	end InternalHom
872	
873	/-! ### The presheaf dual `M^∨ := ℋom(M, R)`
874	
875	The presheaf dual is the internal hom into the monoidal unit (the structure presheaf
876	viewed as a module over itself), assembled in the same single-universe context as
877	`InternalHom.internalHom`. Blueprint `def:presheaf_dual`. -/
878	
879	section Dual
880	
881	open InternalHom Opposite
882	
883	variable {D : Type u} [Category.{u, u} D] {R₀ : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
884	
885	/-- **The presheaf dual `M^∨ := ℋom(M, R)`** (blueprint `def:presheaf_dual`): the
886	internal hom into the monoidal unit `𝟙_` (the structure presheaf `R` viewed as a module
887	over itself / the regular representation). The value
888	`M^∨(U) = ModuleCat.of (R(U)) (M|_U ⟶ R|_U)` is the `R(U)`-module of `R|_U`-linear
889	functionals on `M|_U`, the presheaf-of-modules analogue of the fixed-ring linear dual
890	`Module.Dual R M = (M ⟶ R)` that serves as the `⊗`-inverse of an invertible module.
891	Project-local: Mathlib has the fixed-ring dual but no `PresheafOfModules`-level dual for
892	the varying structure sheaf. -/
893	noncomputable def dual
894	    (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
895	    PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :=
896	  InternalHom.internalHom M (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
897	
898	/-- The evaluation functional `φ ↦ (s ↦ φ(s))` of a dual section `φ`, cast to an
899	`R₀.obj X`-linear map `M(X) → R(X)` (the over-category ring at the terminal section is
900	definitionally `R₀.obj X`). Helper for `internalHomEvalApp`. -/
901	noncomputable def evalLin
902	    (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) (X : Dᵒᵖ)
903	    (φ : (dual M).obj X) :
904	    (M.obj X : Type u) →ₗ[(R₀.obj X : Type u)] (R₀.obj X : Type u) :=
905	  ((φ : restr X.unop M ⟶ restr X.unop
906	      (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))).app
907	        (op (Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)))).hom
908	
909	/-- `evalLin` is additive in the dual section `φ` (the dual-section addition is the
910	categorical `Hom`-addition, on which `app`/`hom`-application is definitionally additive). -/
911	lemma evalLin_add
912	    (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) (X : Dᵒᵖ)
913	    (φ φ' : (dual M).obj X) :
914	    evalLin M X (φ + φ') = evalLin M X φ + evalLin M X φ' :=
915	  LinearMap.ext fun _ => rfl
916	
917	/-- `evalLin` is `R₀.obj X`-linear in the dual section `φ`, using the `homModule`
918	action `c • φ = φ ≫ globalSMul c` and `termRingMap_terminal`. -/
919	lemma evalLin_smul
920	    (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) (X : Dᵒᵖ)
921	    (c : (R₀.obj X : Type u)) (φ : (dual M).obj X) :
922	    evalLin M X (c • φ) = c • evalLin M X φ := by
923	  refine LinearMap.ext fun s => ?_
924	  rw [LinearMap.smul_apply]
925	  change ((ConcreteCategory.hom
926	      (termRingMap (R := (Over.forget X.unop).op ⋙ R₀) Over.mkIdTerminal
927	        (op (Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)))) c)
928	    • ((φ : restr X.unop M ⟶ restr X.unop
929	        (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))).app
930	          (op (Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)))).hom s : (R₀.obj X : Type u))
931	    = c • evalLin M X φ s
932	  rw [termRingMap_terminal]
933	  rfl
934	
935	/-- **The open-by-open evaluation/contraction map** underlying `internalHomEval`. At an
936	object `X` it is the `R(X)`-bilinear contraction
937	`(M(X)) ⊗_{R(X)} (M|_X ⟶ R|_X) → R(X)`, `s ⊗ φ ↦ φ(s)`, where `φ(s)` evaluates the
938	functional `φ` (a morphism of restricted presheaves of modules over `Over X.unop`) at its
939	terminal section, applied to `s`. Bilinearity over `R(X)`: linearity in `s` is linearity
940	of `evalLin`; linearity in `φ` is `evalLin_add` / `evalLin_smul`. The codomain is cast to
941	`R₀.obj X` (defeq to the unit value `(𝟙_).obj X`) to pin the `CommRingCat` module. -/
942	noncomputable def internalHomEvalApp
943	    (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) (X : Dᵒᵖ) :
944	    (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj M (dual M)).obj X ⟶
945	      (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))).obj X :=
946	  show ModuleCat.of (R₀.obj X : Type u)
947	        (TensorProduct (R₀.obj X : Type u) (M.obj X) ((dual M).obj X))

220	`S`-module `M`, the `R`-linear equivalence
221	`restrictScalars_e (M →ₗ[S] S) ≃ₗ[R] (restrictScalars_e M →ₗ[R] R)`, `φ ↦ e.symm ∘ φ`.
222	
223	This is the ModuleCat-level **H2′ ingredient** of the (still-open, d.2-FREE) C-bridge
224	`(dual M).restrict f ≅ dual (M.restrict f)` along an open immersion `f`: the C-bridge
225	mirrors `tensorObj_restrict_iso` exactly (Step 1 `restrictFunctorIsoPullback`, Step 2
226	`sheafificationCompPullback`, Step 3 strip sheafification, Step 4 H1
227	`pushforwardPushforwardAdj`∘`leftAdjointUniq` ∘ this H2′), with `⊗` replaced by `dual`
228	and `restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv` replaced by this equivalence. Like its tensor
229	counterpart it is **pure algebra over a ring iso — no stalk, no filtered-colimit, no
230	whiskering of the sheafification unit (no d.2)**: a map `M → S` is `R`-linear (via `e`)
231	iff it is `S`-linear, and `e.symm` carries the codomain `S` to `R`. The forward map is
232	`R`-linear; the inverse `ψ ↦ e ∘ ψ` is its two-sided inverse. The `iMS` `Module R`
233	instance on `M →ₗ[S] S` is base change along `e`. -/
234	noncomputable def restrictScalarsRingIsoDualEquiv (e : R ≃+* S)
235	    (M : Type u) [AddCommGroup M] [Module S M] :
236	    letI _iM : Module R M := Module.compHom M e.toRingHom
237	    letI _iMS : Module R (M →ₗ[S] S) := Module.compHom _ e.toRingHom
238	    (M →ₗ[S] S) ≃ₗ[R] (M →ₗ[R] R) := by
239	  letI _iM : Module R M := Module.compHom M e.toRingHom
240	  letI _iMS : Module R (M →ₗ[S] S) := Module.compHom _ e.toRingHom
241	  have hsmulM : ∀ (r : R) (m : M), r • m = e r • m := fun _ _ => rfl
242	  refine
243	    { toFun := fun φ =>
244	        { toFun := fun m => e.symm (φ m)
245	          map_add' := fun m m' => by simp [map_add]
246	          map_smul' := fun r m => by
247	            simp only [RingHom.id_apply, smul_eq_mul]
248	            rw [hsmulM, map_smul, smul_eq_mul, map_mul, e.symm_apply_apply] }
249	      invFun := fun ψ =>
250	        { toFun := fun m => e (ψ m)
251	          map_add' := fun m m' => by simp [map_add]
252	          map_smul' := fun s m => by
253	            simp only [RingHom.id_apply, smul_eq_mul]
254	            have hsm : s • m = (e.symm s) • m := by rw [hsmulM, e.apply_symm_apply]
255	            rw [hsm, map_smul, smul_eq_mul, map_mul, e.apply_symm_apply] }
256	      left_inv := fun φ => by ext m; exact e.apply_symm_apply _
257	      right_inv := fun ψ => by ext m; exact e.symm_apply_apply _
258	      map_add' := fun φ φ' => by
259	        ext m
260	        simp only [LinearMap.add_apply, LinearMap.coe_mk, AddHom.coe_mk, map_add]
261	      map_smul' := fun r φ => by
262	        ext m
263	        simp only [RingHom.id_apply, LinearMap.smul_apply, LinearMap.coe_mk, AddHom.coe_mk,
264	          smul_eq_mul]
265	        change e.symm ((e r • φ) m) = r * e.symm (φ m)
266	        rw [LinearMap.smul_apply, smul_eq_mul, map_mul, e.symm_apply_apply] }
267	
268	end RestrictScalarsRingIsoTensor
269	
270	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — `restrictScalars` is lax monoidal
271	
272	The presheaf-of-modules restriction-of-scalars functor along a morphism of
273	presheaves of *commutative* rings is lax monoidal. Mathlib ships the sectionwise
274	fact `ModuleCat.restrictScalars f` is `LaxMonoidal`
275	(`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Monoidal.Adjunction`); here we lift it to
276	the presheaf level through the sectionwise presheaf monoidal structure
277	(`PresheafOfModules.Monoidal`). This is the sole project-side ingredient feeding
278	the oplax comparison `δ` of `pullback φ` (the mate of `pushforward φ`) used to
279	close `tensorObj_restrict_iso`. Per blueprint `lem:restrictscalars_laxmonoidal`. -/
280	
281	namespace PresheafOfModules
282	
283	universe v'
284	
285	variable {C : Type u} [Category.{v'} C] {R S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
286	
287	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
288	/-- The lax-monoidal unit `ε` of `restrictScalars α`, assembled sectionwise from
289	`ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app X)`'s lax-monoidal unit. -/
290	noncomputable def restrictScalarsLaxε
291	    (α : R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat ⟶ S ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :
292	    (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))) ⟶
293	      (restrictScalars α).obj (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (S ⋙ forget₂ _ _))) where
294	  app X := Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app X).hom)
295	  naturality {X Y} f := by
296	    ext r
297	    dsimp
298	    erw [PresheafOfModules.unit_map_one, ModuleCat.restrictScalars_η,
299	      ModuleCat.restrictScalars_η]
300	    simp only [map_one]
301	    erw [PresheafOfModules.unit_map_one]
302	
303	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
304	/-- The lax-monoidal tensorator `μ` of `restrictScalars α`, assembled sectionwise
305	from `ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app X)`'s lax-monoidal tensorator. -/
306	noncomputable def restrictScalarsLaxμ
307	    (α : R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat ⟶ S ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)
308	    (M₁ M₂ : PresheafOfModules.{u} (S ⋙ forget₂ _ _)) :
309	    (restrictScalars α).obj M₁ ⊗ (restrictScalars α).obj M₂ ⟶
310	      (restrictScalars α).obj (M₁ ⊗ M₂) where
311	  app X := by
312	    exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.μ (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app X).hom) (M₁.obj X) (M₂.obj X)
313	  naturality {X Y} f := by
314	    refine ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.tensor_ext (fun m₁ m₂ ↦ ?_)
315	    dsimp
316	    erw [PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj_map_tmul, ModuleCat.restrictScalars_μ_tmul,
317	      ModuleCat.restrictScalars_μ_tmul, PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj_map_tmul]
318	    rfl
319	
320	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
321	/-- **`restrictScalars α` is lax monoidal** for a morphism `α` of presheaves of
322	commutative rings. Project-local lift of `ModuleCat.instLaxMonoidalRestrictScalars`. -/
323	noncomputable instance restrictScalarsLaxMonoidal
324	    (α : R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat ⟶ S ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :
325	    (PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α).LaxMonoidal where
326	  ε := restrictScalarsLaxε α
327	  μ M₁ M₂ := restrictScalarsLaxμ α M₁ M₂
328	  μ_natural_left := by
329	    intro X Y f X'
330	    ext1 Z
331	    exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.μ_natural_left (F := ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app Z).hom)
332	      (f.app Z) (X'.obj Z)
333	  μ_natural_right := by
334	    intro X Y X' f
335	    ext1 Z
336	    exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.μ_natural_right (F := ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app Z).hom)
337	      (X'.obj Z) (f.app Z)
338	  associativity := by
339	    intro M N P
340	    ext1 Z
341	    exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.associativity (F := ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app Z).hom)
342	      (M.obj Z) (N.obj Z) (P.obj Z)
343	  left_unitality := by
344	    intro M
345	    ext1 Z
346	    exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.left_unitality (F := ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app Z).hom)
347	      (M.obj Z)
348	  right_unitality := by
349	    intro M
350	    ext1 Z
351	    exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.right_unitality (F := ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app Z).hom)
352	      (M.obj Z)
353	
354	end PresheafOfModules
355	
356	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the presheaf-level pushforward adjunction (H1)
357	
358	De-sheafification of `SheafOfModules.{pushforwardNatTrans, pushforwardCongr,
359	pushforwardPushforwardAdj}`
360	(`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Sheaf/PushforwardContinuous.lean`, L154/L73/L226) to the
361	`PresheafOfModules` level. Every line of the sheaf template already manipulates
362	`.val`/`.val.presheaf` presheaf data, so the de-sheafification is mechanical (drop the `Sheaf`
363	wrapper and the sheaf-only `IsContinuous` `letI`s). These are the **H1** linchpin of
364	`tensorObj_restrict_iso`: from a pair `adj : F ⊣ G` one obtains a presheaf-level adjunction
365	`pushforward φ ⊣ pushforward ψ`, hence — against the existing
366	`PresheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction` and via `Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq` —
367	the iso `pushforward β ≅ pullback φ` that moves the abstract presheaf pullback onto the concrete
368	restriction pushforward. Per blueprint `lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso`, Step 3 (the H1 residual). -/
369	
370	namespace PresheafOfModules
371	
372	open CategoryTheory Functor
373	
374	section PushforwardNatTrans
375	
376	universe v₁ v₂ uC uD
377	
378	variable {C : Type uC} [Category.{v₁} C] {D : Type uD} [Category.{v₂} D]
379	  {F G : C ⥤ D} {S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}}
380	  (φ : S ⟶ G.op ⋙ R)
381	
382	/-- **Presheaf-level `pushforwardNatTrans`.** A natural transformation `α : F ⟶ G` of functors
383	`C ⥤ D` induces a natural transformation between the pushforward functors along `F` and `G`.
384	De-sheafification of `SheafOfModules.pushforwardNatTrans`
385	(`Sheaf/PushforwardContinuous.lean:154`), dropping the `.val` wrapper. -/
386	noncomputable def pushforwardNatTrans (α : F ⟶ G) :
387	    pushforward.{u} φ ⟶ pushforward.{u} (φ ≫ Functor.whiskerRight (NatTrans.op α) R) where
388	  app M :=
389	    { app := fun U => (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (φ.app U).hom).map (M.map (α.app U.unop).op)
390	      naturality := fun {U V} i => by
391	        ext x
392	        dsimp
393	        change (M.presheaf.map (G.map i.unop).op ≫ M.presheaf.map (α.app V.unop).op) _ =
394	          (M.presheaf.map (α.app U.unop).op ≫ M.presheaf.map (F.map i.unop).op) _

593	/-- The section-wise action of `globalSMul f`: at `Y`, it is scalar multiplication by
594	`termRingMap Y f`. -/
595	lemma globalSMul_hom_apply (f : (R.obj (Opposite.op T) : Type u)) (Y : Cᵒᵖ) (m : N.obj Y) :
596	    ((globalSMul hT N f).app Y).hom m = ((ConcreteCategory.hom (termRingMap hT Y)) f) • m := rfl
597	
598	/-- `globalSMul 1 = 𝟙`. -/
599	lemma globalSMul_one : globalSMul hT N 1 = 𝟙 N := by
600	  ext Y m; rw [globalSMul_hom_apply, map_one, one_smul]; rfl
601	
602	/-- `globalSMul 0 = 0`. -/
603	lemma globalSMul_zero : globalSMul hT N 0 = 0 := by
604	  ext Y m; rw [globalSMul_hom_apply, map_zero, zero_smul]; rfl
605	
606	/-- `globalSMul` is additive in the scalar. -/
607	lemma globalSMul_add (f g : (R.obj (Opposite.op T) : Type u)) :
608	    globalSMul hT N (f + g) = globalSMul hT N f + globalSMul hT N g := by

611	/-- `globalSMul` turns ring multiplication into composition (the scalar endomorphisms

613	lemma globalSMul_mul (f g : (R.obj (Opposite.op T) : Type u)) :
614	    globalSMul hT N (f * g) = globalSMul hT N g ≫ globalSMul hT N f := by

277	def LinearEquiv.toModuleIso {g₁ : AddCommGroup X₁} {g₂ : AddCommGroup X₂} {m₁ : Module R X₁}

281	  hom_inv_id := by ext; apply e.left_inv
282	  inv_hom_id := by ext; apply e.right_inv
283	
284	namespace CategoryTheory.Iso
285	variable {X Y : ModuleCat R}
286	
287	/-- Build a `LinearEquiv` from an isomorphism in the category `ModuleCat R`. -/
288	def toLinearEquiv (i : X ≅ Y) : X ≃ₗ[R] Y :=
289	  .ofLinear i.hom.hom i.inv.hom (by aesop) (by aesop)
290	
291	@[simp] lemma toLinearEquiv_apply (i : X ≅ Y) (x : X) : i.toLinearEquiv x = i.hom x := rfl

293	@[simp] lemma toLinearMap_toLinearEquiv (i : X ≅ Y) : i.toLinearEquiv = i.hom.hom := rfl
294	
295	end CategoryTheory.Iso
296

125:noncomputable instance monoidalCategory :

variable (M₁ M₂ M₃ M₄ : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
/-- Auxiliary definition for `tensorObj`. -/
noncomputable def tensorObjMap {X Y : Cᵒᵖ} (f : X ⟶ Y) : M₁.obj X ⊗ M₂.obj X ⟶
    (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (R.map f).hom).obj (M₁.obj Y ⊗ M₂.obj Y) :=
  ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.tensorLift (fun m₁ m₂ ↦ M₁.map f m₁ ⊗ₜ M₂.map f m₂)
    (by
      intro m₁ m₁' m₂
      dsimp +instances
      rw [map_add, TensorProduct.add_tmul])
    (by intro a m₁ m₂; dsimp; erw [M₁.map_smul]; rfl)
    (by
      intro m₁ m₂ m₂'
      dsimp +instances
      rw [map_add, TensorProduct.tmul_add])
    (by intro a m₁ m₂; dsimp; erw [M₂.map_smul, TensorProduct.tmul_smul (r := R.map f a)]; rfl)

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
/-- The tensor product of two presheaves of modules. -/
@[simps obj]
noncomputable def tensorObj : PresheafOfModules (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _) where
  obj X := M₁.obj X ⊗ M₂.obj X
  map f := tensorObjMap M₁ M₂ f
  map_id X := ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.tensor_ext (by
    intro m₁ m₂
    dsimp [tensorObjMap]
    simp
    rfl) -- `ModuleCat.restrictScalarsId'App_inv_apply` doesn't get picked up due to type mismatch
  map_comp f g := ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.tensor_ext (by
    intro m₁ m₂
    dsimp [tensorObjMap]
    simp +instances)

variable {M₁ M₂ M₃ M₄}

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
@[simp]
lemma tensorObj_map_tmul {X Y : Cᵒᵖ} (f : X ⟶ Y) (m₁ : M₁.obj X) (m₂ : M₂.obj X) :
    DFunLike.coe (α := (M₁.obj X ⊗ M₂.obj X :))
      (β := fun _ ↦ (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (R.map f).hom).obj (M₁.obj Y ⊗ M₂.obj Y))
      (ModuleCat.Hom.hom (R := ↑(R.obj X)) ((tensorObj M₁ M₂).map f)) (m₁ ⊗ₜ[R.obj X] m₂) =
    M₁.map f m₁ ⊗ₜ[R.obj Y] M₂.map f m₂ := rfl

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
/-- The tensor product of two morphisms of presheaves of modules. -/
@[simps]
noncomputable def tensorHom (f : M₁ ⟶ M₂) (g : M₃ ⟶ M₄) : tensorObj M₁ M₃ ⟶ tensorObj M₂ M₄ where
  app X := f.app X ⊗ₘ g.app X
  naturality {X Y} φ := ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.tensor_ext (fun m₁ m₃ ↦ by
    dsimp
    rw [tensorObj_map_tmul]
    -- Need `erw` because of the type mismatch in `map` and the tensor product.
    erw [ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.tensorHom_tmul, tensorObj_map_tmul]
    rw [naturality_apply, naturality_apply]
    simp)

end Monoidal

open Monoidal

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
open ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory in
noncomputable instance monoidalCategoryStruct :
    MonoidalCategoryStruct (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)) where
  tensorObj := tensorObj
  whiskerLeft _ _ _ g := tensorHom (𝟙 _) g
  whiskerRight f _ := tensorHom f (𝟙 _)
  tensorHom := tensorHom
  tensorUnit := unit _
  associator M₁ M₂ M₃ := isoMk (fun _ ↦ α_ _ _ _)
    (fun _ _ _ ↦ ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.tensor_ext₃' (by intros; rfl))
  leftUnitor M := Iso.symm (isoMk (fun _ ↦ (λ_ _).symm) (fun X Y f ↦ by
    ext m
    dsimp [CommRingCat.forgetToRingCat_obj]
    erw [leftUnitor_inv_apply, leftUnitor_inv_apply, tensorObj_map_tmul, (R.map f).hom.map_one]
    rfl))
  rightUnitor M := Iso.symm (isoMk (fun _ ↦ (ρ_ _).symm) (fun X Y f ↦ by
    ext m
    dsimp [CommRingCat.forgetToRingCat_obj]
    erw [rightUnitor_inv_apply, rightUnitor_inv_apply, tensorObj_map_tmul, (R.map f).hom.map_one]
    rfl))

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
noncomputable instance monoidalCategory :

156	lemma restrictScalars_isIso_μ (e : R ≃+* S) (M₁ M₂ : ModuleCat.{u} S) :
157	    IsIso (Functor.LaxMonoidal.μ (ModuleCat.restrictScalars e.toRingHom) M₁ M₂) := by

159	  have hfun : ⇑(Functor.LaxMonoidal.μ (ModuleCat.restrictScalars e.toRingHom) M₁ M₂)
160	      = ⇑(restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv e M₁ M₂) := by
161	    ext z
162	    induction z using TensorProduct.induction_on with
163	    | zero => rw [map_zero]; exact (map_zero _).symm
164	    | tmul a b =>
165	        erw [ModuleCat.restrictScalars_μ_tmul]
166	        exact (restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv_apply_tmul e M₁ M₂ a b).symm
167	    | add x y hx hy => rw [map_add, hx, hy]; exact (map_add _ x y).symm
168	  rw [hfun]
169	  exact (restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv e M₁ M₂).bijective

171	/-- **The lax-monoidal unit `ε` of `restrictScalars` along a ring iso is an
172	isomorphism.** Its underlying map is the ring map `e` (`ModuleCat.restrictScalars_η`),
173	which is bijective since `e` is a ring equivalence. -/
174	lemma restrictScalars_isIso_ε (e : R ≃+* S) :
175	    IsIso (Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε (ModuleCat.restrictScalars e.toRingHom)) := by
176	  rw [ConcreteCategory.isIso_iff_bijective]
177	  have hfun : ⇑(Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε (ModuleCat.restrictScalars e.toRingHom)) = ⇑e := by
178	    ext r
179	    exact ModuleCat.restrictScalars_η (f := e.toRingHom) r
180	  rw [hfun]
181	  exact e.bijective
182	
183	/-- **`ModuleCat.restrictScalars` along a ring isomorphism is (strong) monoidal.**
184	The packaged `Functor.Monoidal` structure obtained from the lax structure by
185	inverting `ε` (`restrictScalars_isIso_ε`) and `μ` (`restrictScalars_isIso_μ`). This
186	is the clean, reusable strong-monoidal upgrade that Mathlib provides for
187	`extendScalars` but not `restrictScalars`; it is the ModuleCat-level core of the H2
188	ingredient of `tensorObj_restrict_iso`. -/
189	@[implicit_reducible]
190	noncomputable def restrictScalarsMonoidalOfRingEquiv (e : R ≃+* S) :
191	    (ModuleCat.restrictScalars e.toRingHom).Monoidal := by

# Picard/TensorObjSubstrate/DualInverse.lean

## sliceDualTransport (L184) — ROUTE-2, partial build (IN PROGRESS)

### Approach
Route-2 sanctioned: build the per-`V` `𝒪_Y(V)`-linear iso
`((pushforward β).obj (dual M.val)).obj V ≅ (dual ((pushforward β).obj M.val)).obj V`
by hand = leg-A (slice-Hom base-change reindex across `f.opensFunctor`) ∘ leg-B
(`restrictScalarsRingIsoDualEquiv`-style ring-iso codomain swap).

### Result: PARTIAL — real code progress; not closed.

**Walls resolved (genuine code, all compiling):**

1. **Module-instance synthesis wall (the planner-flagged "instance-delicate" point).**
   `refine LinearEquiv.toModuleIso ?_; refine { toFun := … }` FAILS: the structure literal
   re-synthesizes `Module 𝒪_Y(V) (carrier)` on the **`pushforward₀`-reduced** LHS carrier and cannot
   find it (the `𝒪_Y(V)`-structure lives on the un-reduced `(pushforward β).obj …` / `restrictScalars`
   form). FIX, now in code:
   - `set β : Y.ringCatSheaf.obj ⟶ (Hom.opensFunctor f).op ⋙ X.ringCatSheaf.obj := …` folds the goal
     to `(pushforward β …) ≅ (dual (pushforward β …))` (the lets were pre-inlined; `extract_lets`
     makes no progress, but `set` with the explicit `whiskerRight {app…}` term re-folds it).
   - `letI lhsMod := inferInstance` (LHS, `pushforward β`-form) and
     `letI rhsMod := InternalHom.internalHomObjModule (R := Y.presheaf) V.unop (pushforward β M.val) (𝟙_ _)`
     (RHS — `inferInstance` does NOT find this one; the dual's section module is `internalHomObjModule`).
   - `refine LinearEquiv.toModuleIso (m₁ := lhsMod) (m₂ := rhsMod) ?_` — the `m₁`/`m₂` MUST be passed
     explicitly; otherwise they are deferred metavars re-synthesized (and failing) at the structure literal.

2. **toFun leg-A BUILT and typechecks.** The forward map's component at `W : (Over V.unop)ᵒᵖ` is

23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. Supports DeepSeek, Kimi (OpenAI-compatible via `--provider kimi`), Kimi (Anthropic-compatible via `--provider kimi-anthropic`), OpenRouter, OpenAI, and Gemini; defaults to `--provider auto` which picks the best available key automatically. **Requires at least one key in env** (`DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` / `MOONSHOT_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY`) — check via `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. Both `kimi` and `kimi-anthropic` use the same `MOONSHOT_API_KEY`; the latter speaks Moonshot's Anthropic-compatible Messages endpoint and supports `--think`. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).

When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.

35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \

42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | read | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | — (review owns) | — | **write** (reset at review start) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	## Agent Roles
112	
113	### Plan Agent
114	
115	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
116	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
117	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
118	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
119	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
120	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
121	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
122	
123	### Prover Agent
124	
125	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
126	- Your **active mode** is injected directly into your invocation prompt — read it carefully, it defines your goal, workflow, and constraints for this session.
127	- When your mode specifies `read_blueprint: true`, you are permitted to **read** your assigned blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<your_slug>.tex`) for the informal proof sketch. You may never write to blueprint chapters.
128	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
129	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
130	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
131	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
132	
133	### Subagents
134	
135	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
136	
137	### Review Agent
138	
139	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
140	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
141	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
142	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
143	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
144	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
145	
146	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
147	
148	These run automatically between phases:
149	
150	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
151	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
152	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.
153

1	# Prover — Prover Stage
2	
3	You fill `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs in your assigned `.lean` file.
4	
5	## Workflow
6	
7	1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
8	2. Read `task_pending.md` for prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas on your file.
9	3. Check the `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments (file-specific user hints).
10	4. **Read the relevant blueprint chapter before writing Lean code.** The chapter holds the mathematical proof sketch you must align with. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
11	5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs. Push as far as possible.
12	6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you can't fully close a sorry, leave your best attempt — commented-out steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` if needed), but your work must be visible for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to a bare `sorry` — that wastes the work.
13	7. Write your results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
14	
15	**Write permissions**: only your assigned `.lean` file(s) and `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, blueprint chapters, or other agents' files.
16	
17	## Protected declarations
18	
19	`archon-protected.yaml` lists declarations with **frozen signatures**. You may fill their proof bodies but must not rename, re-type, reorder arguments, or weaken hypotheses. Only the mathematician edits protected signatures.
20	
21	## Avoid early termination
22	
23	- Don't abandon a proof prematurely. Many complex proofs run to thousands of lines.
24	- Difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave a `sorry`.
25	- Don't delegate to "the next iteration" or "another prover" if more effort could close it.
26	- Only modify the proof for your assigned task — leave unrelated proofs untouched.
27	- **Decompose**: break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure when available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each individually until the entire goal closes.
28	
29	## Task completion criteria
30	
31	Your task is complete ONLY when ALL of:
32	
33	1. Every `sorry` in scope is replaced with a complete proof.
34	2. Zero axioms introduced.
35	3. The file compiles cleanly.
36	
37	## Never weaken the type to dodge the proof
38	
39	When the substantive type is unattainable this iter, you have one honest move: leave `sorry` with the **intended type signature** so the type itself encodes the claim. You must NOT substitute a tautologically-true type to trivialize the body. Three patterns are banned:
40	
41	- **Reflexive-iso placeholder.** Replacing a non-trivial `X ≅ Y` (or `Nonempty (X ≅ Y)`) target with `Nonempty (X ≅ X) := ⟨Iso.refl _⟩`. The type now asserts nothing; the docstring's "iter-N placeholder" disclosure does not undo the structural lie, and the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference will resolve to this hollow declaration.
42	- **`Classical.choice` body around an explicit witness.** Writing `Classical.choice (α := X) ⟨witness⟩` to dissolve `Type` vs `Prop` friction — the witness is unreachable through any unfold, so the def is indistinguishable from `Classical.choice ⟨any-other-X⟩` downstream. Either use `Classical.choose` on the existential chain (preserves the spec), or `Nonempty.some`-with-witness; or ship a typed `sorry`.
43	- **Empty-content `proof_wanted`.** `proof_wanted` discards the declaration post-elaboration — the named decl never enters the environment, so the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference breaks. Use `theorem name : T := by sorry` with the intended signature instead.
44	
45	**Litmus test**: if you `unfold` your declaration, does it expose the named substantive content (Kähler differential module, the explicit iso, …) or does it stop at `Classical.choice` / `Iso.refl _` / nothing? If the latter, the body is structurally vacuous — ship the typed `sorry` instead.
46	
47	## When infrastructure is missing
48	
49	Do NOT report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up:
50	
51	1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`): "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.
52	2. **Try the alternative** — formalize whatever the informal agent suggests.
53	3. **If you still can't**: write the alternative sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md` and record in your task result what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found. "I couldn't prove X, but here's approach Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than "infrastructure missing".
54	
55	When stuck more generally: break into smaller subgoals, search Mathlib more thoroughly, prove missing helpers yourself, try alternative strategies, re-read the blueprint, use Web Search for published proofs.
56	
57	**Impossibility vs difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.
58	
59	## Proof style
60	
61	- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its body.
62	- Keep edits minimal; don't delete comments or change labels; don't add unrelated declarations.
63	- Helper lemmas you introduced may be modified if they turn out wrong.
64	- Add a concise comment above each helper lemma so reuse is easy.
65	- **`change` vs `show`** — `change` reshapes the goal up to defeq; `show` is purely display-level annotation. Using `show` where `change` is needed produces a linter warning. Default to `change` when in doubt.
66	
67	## Mathlib tags in PROGRESS.md
68	
69	The plan agent tags suggested lemmas:
70	
71	- `[verified]` — confirmed to exist.
72	- `[expected]` — guessed by naming convention. Quick `lean_local_search`; pivot if it doesn't exist.
73	- `[gap]` — verified NOT in Mathlib. Don't waste search time; formalize a workaround.
74	
75	## LSP MCP tools
76	
77	The `archon-lean-lsp` server exposes Lean LSP operations as **MCP tool calls** (full names like `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_goal` / `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages`). The lean4 skill reference uses short names (`lean_goal`, `lean_local_search`, …) — same tools, NOT shell binaries.
78	
79	- Always invoke through your tool-call interface, the same way as `Read` or `Grep`.
80	- **Never** call `Bash` with `lean_goal …` etc. — there is no such shell command.
81	- First LSP action: `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages` on your file. If it returns `success: false` the server is cold; retry once or run `lake build` once via Bash, then retry.
82	
83	## Search protocol
84	
85	Follow `references/lean-lsp-tools-api.md`. Key priority:
86	
87	1. `lean_local_search` first.
88	2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — describe the mathematical content, not just the name.
89	3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only.
90	4. Never use shell `find` / `grep` to locate Mathlib theorems.
91	
92	## Tooling traps
93	
94	- **Trust `goals_after`, not just empty diagnostics**: in `lean_multi_attempt`, `diagnostics: []` can be misleading (line-scoping quirks attach errors to the enclosing `by` block). Check that `goals_after` is empty or has advanced.
95	- **Beware `lean_run_code` with imports**: standalone snippets can silently swallow elaboration errors when they contain imports. Rely on the actual file's `lean_diagnostic_messages` for authoritative verification.
96	
97	## Logging
98	
99	Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` (mirror your `.lean` path: `Algebra/WLocal.lean` → `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`):
100	
101	```markdown
102	# Algebra/WLocal.lean
103	
104	## wLocal_iff (line 45)
105	### Attempt 1
106	- **Approach:** Direct case split on maximal ideals
107	- **Result:** FAILED — needed IsLocalRing instance, not available
108	- **Dead end:** direct case split without IsLocalRing
109	
110	### Attempt 2
111	- **Approach:** Stacks 0A31, characterize via bijection on spectra
112	- **Result:** RESOLVED
113	- **Key insight:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective` bridges the gap
114	
115	## helper_bijective (line 78)
116	### Attempt 1
117	- **Approach:** Split into injectivity + surjectivity; prove injectivity
118	- **Result:** PARTIAL (branch closed) — injectivity closed; surjectivity remains
119	- **Next step:** `PrimeSpectrum.range_comap_of_surjective` for the right branch
120	- **Lemmas found:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_surjective`
121	```
122	
123	**Rules**: one section per theorem/lemma in your file. Each attempt records approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / PARTIAL / IN PROGRESS), dead-end warnings or next steps. Log negative search results ("Searched 'projective module infinite rank' — nothing in Mathlib"). The plan agent merges these into `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md` next iter.
124	
125	**Read-only context**: read `task_pending.md` (prior attempts on your file) and `task_done.md` (when the problem resembles a completed one). Do not write to either.
126	
127	## End-of-session handoff
128	
129	Before stopping:
130	
131	1. Write `task_results/<your_file>.md` with: current result, lemmas discovered, concrete next step, dead-end warnings.
132	2. Save all changes; ensure the file compiles (use scoped `sorry` for stuck parts if needed).
133
