1	\chapter{{\v C}ech computation of higher direct images $R^i f_*$ (unconditional)}
2	\label{chap:Cohomology_CechHigherDirectImage}
3	% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/CechHigherDirectImage.lean
4	% SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, \S{\v C}ech cohomology of
5	% quasi-coherent sheaves and \S Quasi-coherence of higher direct images
6	% (Lemmas \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial},
7	% \texttt{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine-cohomology-zero},
8	% \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent},
9	% \texttt{lemma-quasi-coherence-higher-direct-images-application},
10	% \texttt{lemma-flat-base-change-cohomology}) (read from
11	% references/stacks-coherent.tex).
12	
13	\section{Setup and motivation}
14	
15	This chapter constructs the higher derived direct images \(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\) for
16	\(i \geq 1\) \emph{without appealing to injective resolutions in the category of
17	sheaves of modules}. The companion development of
18	Chapter~\ref{chap:Cohomology_HigherDirectImage} defines \(R^i f_*\) as a right
19	derived functor, which in a formalisation requires the ambient category of
20	\(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules to have enough injectives; that property is not currently
21	available for sheaves of modules over a sheaf of rings whose value category varies
22	over the site. The {\v C}ech approach developed here sidesteps the issue entirely:
23	it computes \(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\) as the cohomology of an explicit complex built
24	from the pushforwards of \(\mathcal{F}\) over the finite intersections of an affine
25	open cover, and so produces an \emph{unconditional} construction of \(R^i f_*\) for
26	quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{F}\) and separated quasi-compact \(f\).
27	
28	Throughout, \(f : X \to S\) is a morphism of schemes that is quasi-compact and
29	separated (so that all finite intersections of an affine open cover of \(X\) are
30	again affine), and \(\mathcal{F}\) is a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module. We
31	write \(\operatorname{QCoh}(X)\) for the category of quasi-coherent
32	\(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules. A base change of \(f\) along a morphism \(g : S' \to S\)
33	is recorded by the cartesian square obtained from the fibre product
34	\(X' = X \times_S S'\):
35	\[
36	  \begin{array}{ccc}
37	    X' & \xrightarrow{\;g'\;} & X \\[2pt]
38	    {\scriptstyle f'}\big\downarrow & & \big\downarrow{\scriptstyle f} \\[2pt]
39	    S' & \xrightarrow{\;g\;} & S
40	  \end{array}
41	\]
42	Here \(f' : X' \to S'\) is the base change of \(f\), \(g' : X' \to X\) is the first
43	projection, and \(\mathcal{F}' = (g')^*\mathcal{F}\) is the pullback of
44	\(\mathcal{F}\) to \(X'\).
45	
46	\section{The {\v C}ech nerve and {\v C}ech complex of an affine cover}
47	
48	\begin{definition}
49	[{\v C}ech nerve of an affine open cover]
50	  \label{def:cech_nerve}
51	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.CechNerve}
52	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, \S{\v C}ech cohomology of
53	  % quasi-coherent sheaves, opening paragraph and Lemma
54	  % \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial}
55	  % (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L36--67).
56	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $X$ be a scheme.
57	  % Let $U \subset X$ be an affine open.
58	  % Recall that a {\it standard open covering} of $U$ is a covering
59	  % of the form $\mathcal{U} : U = \bigcup_{i = 1}^n D(f_i)$
60	  % where $f_1, \ldots, f_n \in \Gamma(U, \mathcal{O}_X)$ generate
61	  % the unit ideal, see
62	  % Schemes, Definition \ref{schemes-definition-standard-covering}."
63	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, \S{\v C}ech cohomology of
64	  quasi-coherent sheaves.}
65	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and let
66	  \(\mathcal{U} : X = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) be a finite open cover of \(X\) by
67	  affine opens, with index set \(I\). For a finite nonempty tuple
68	  \(i_0, \ldots, i_p \in I\) write
69	  \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p} = U_{i_0} \cap \cdots \cap U_{i_p}\) for the corresponding
70	  intersection. The \emph{{\v C}ech nerve} of \(\mathcal{U}\) is the augmented
71	  simplicial object in \(\operatorname{QCoh}(X)\) whose object in simplicial degree
72	  \(p\) is the direct image, along the open immersions
73	  \(j_{i_0 \ldots i_p} : U_{i_0 \ldots i_p} \hookrightarrow X\), of the restriction
74	  of \(\mathcal{O}_X\) (or of any chosen quasi-coherent sheaf) to the
75	  \((p+1)\)-fold intersections,
76	  \[
77	    [p] \;\longmapsto\;
78	      \prod_{(i_0, \ldots, i_p) \in I^{p+1}}
79	        (j_{i_0 \ldots i_p})_* \,\bigl(\,\cdot\,|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}}\bigr),
80	  \]
81	  with faces given by the restriction maps that omit one index and degeneracies by
82	  the maps that repeat one index; the augmentation in degree \(-1\) is the object
83	  on all of \(X\). When \(X\) is separated each intersection
84	  \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\) is itself affine. The local affine model is the
85	  \emph{standard open covering} \(U = \bigcup_{i=1}^n D(f_i)\) of an affine open
86	  \(U = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\) by basic opens, where \(f_1, \ldots, f_n \in A\)
87	  generate the unit ideal; on such a cover the intersections are again basic opens
88	  \(D(f_{i_0} \cdots f_{i_p})\).
89	\end{definition}
90	
91	\begin{definition}
92	[{\v C}ech complex of a quasi-coherent sheaf]
93	  \label{def:cech_complex}
94	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.CechComplex}
95	  \uses{def:cech_nerve}
96	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
97	  % \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (proof)
98	  % (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L59--77).
99	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Clearly the {\v C}ech complex
100	  % $\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})$
101	  % is identified with the complex
102	  % $$
103	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to
104	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}} \to
105	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1i_2} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}f_{i_2}} \to
106	  % \ldots
107	  % $$
108	  % We are asked to show that the extended complex
109	  % \begin{equation}
110	  % \label{equation-extended}
111	  % 0 \to
112	  % M \to
113	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to
114	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}} \to
115	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1i_2} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}f_{i_2}} \to
116	  % \ldots
117	  % \end{equation}
118	  % (whose truncation we have studied in
119	  % Algebra, Lemma \ref{algebra-lemma-cover-module}) is exact."
120	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
121	  \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (standard-cover {\v C}ech
122	  vanishing).}
123	  Let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module and let
124	  \(\mathcal{U} : X = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) be a finite affine open cover with
125	  all intersections \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\) affine (e.g.\ \(X\) separated). The
126	  \emph{{\v C}ech complex} \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})\)
127	  is the cochain complex obtained by applying the global-sections functor to the
128	  {\v C}ech nerve of Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve} evaluated at \(\mathcal{F}\);
129	  in degree \(p\) it is
130	  \[
131	    \check{\mathcal{C}}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})
132	      = \prod_{(i_0, \ldots, i_p) \in I^{p+1}}
133	        \mathcal{F}\bigl(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\bigr),
134	  \]
135	  with differential \(d : \check{\mathcal{C}}^p \to \check{\mathcal{C}}^{p+1}\) the
136	  alternating sum \((d s)_{i_0 \ldots i_{p+1}} = \sum_{j=0}^{p+1} (-1)^j\,
137	  s_{i_0 \ldots \widehat{i_j} \ldots i_{p+1}}|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_{p+1}}}\) of the
138	  restriction maps. Over an affine \(U = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\) with
139	  \(\mathcal{F}|_U = \widetilde{M}\) and a standard cover by the
140	  \(D(f_i)\), this is the complex
141	  \(\prod_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to \prod_{i_0 i_1} M_{f_{i_0} f_{i_1}} \to \cdots\) of
142	  localisations. The \emph{relative {\v C}ech complex} \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet
143	  (\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F})\) for \(f : X \to S\) is the analogous complex in
144	  \(\operatorname{QCoh}(S)\) obtained by applying \(f_*\) over each finite
145	  intersection instead of global sections; its terms are
146	  \(\prod_{i_0 \ldots i_p} (f|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}})_*
147	  \bigl(\mathcal{F}|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}}\bigr)\), and it is a complex in
148	  \(\operatorname{QCoh}(S)\) because each \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\) is affine and the
149	  pushforward of a quasi-coherent sheaf along a quasi-compact quasi-separated
150	  morphism is quasi-coherent.
151	\end{definition}
152	
153	\section{Affine acyclicity of the {\v C}ech complex}
154	
155	\begin{lemma}
156	[{\v C}ech acyclicity on affines]
157	  \label{lem:cech_acyclic_affine}
158	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.CechAcyclic.affine}
159	  \uses{def:cech_complex}
160	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Tag 02KG (affine vanishing);
161	  % Lemma \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (standard-cover
162	  % {\v C}ech vanishing) and Lemma
163	  % \texttt{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine-cohomology-zero} (Serre vanishing on affines)
164	  % (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L44--52 and L145--155).
165	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $X$ be a scheme.
166	  % Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-module.
167	  % Let $\mathcal{U} : U = \bigcup_{i = 1}^n D(f_i)$ be a standard
168	  % open covering of an affine open of $X$.
169	  % Then $\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) = 0$ for
170	  % all $p > 0$."
171	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Serre vanishing: Higher cohomology vanishes on affine schemes
172	  % for quasi-coherent modules.
173	  % Let $X$ be a scheme.
174	  % Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-module.
175	  % For any affine open $U \subset X$ we have
176	  % $H^p(U, \mathcal{F}) = 0$ for all $p > 0$."
177	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
178	  \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (standard-cover {\v C}ech
179	  vanishing) and Stacks, Tag 02KG
180	  (\texttt{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine-cohomology-zero}, Serre vanishing on
181	  affines).}
182	  Let \(U = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\) be an affine scheme, \(\mathcal{F}\) a
183	  quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_U\)-module, and
184	  \(\mathcal{U} : U = \bigcup_{i=1}^n D(f_i)\) a standard open cover (so the
185	  \(f_i \in A\) generate the unit ideal). Then the {\v C}ech complex
186	  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})\) has vanishing cohomology
187	  in all positive degrees:
188	  \[
189	    \check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) = 0 \qquad \text{for all } p > 0,
190	  \]
191	  and consequently \(H^p(U, \mathcal{F}) = 0\) for all \(p > 0\).
192	\end{lemma}
193	
194	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "Write $U = \Spec(A)$ for some ring $A$.
195	% In other words, $f_1, \ldots, f_n$ are elements of $A$
196	% which generate the unit ideal of $A$.
197	% Write $\mathcal{F}|_U = \widetilde{M}$ for some $A$-module $M$.
198	% ...
199	% It suffices to show that (\ref{equation-extended})
200	% is exact after localizing at a prime $\mathfrak p$, see
201	% Algebra, Lemma \ref{algebra-lemma-characterize-zero-local}.
202	% In fact we will show that the extended complex localized
203	% at $\mathfrak p$ is homotopic to zero.
204	% There exists an index $i$ such that $f_i \not \in \mathfrak p$.
205	% Choose and fix such an element $i_{\text{fix}}$. ...
206	% Let us define a homotopy
207	% $$
208	% h :
209	% \prod\nolimits_{i_0 \ldots i_{p + 1}}
210	% M_{f_{i_0} \ldots f_{i_{p + 1}}, \mathfrak p}
211	% \longrightarrow
212	% \prod\nolimits_{i_0 \ldots i_p}
213	% M_{f_{i_0} \ldots f_{i_p}, \mathfrak p}
214	% $$
215	% by the rule
216	% $$
217	% h(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_p} = s_{i_{\text{fix}} i_0 \ldots i_p}
218	% $$
219	% ... We compute ... $(dh + hd)(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_p} = s_{i_0 \ldots i_p}$ ...
220	% This proves the identity map is homotopic to zero as desired."
221	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "We are going to apply
222	% Cohomology, Lemma \ref{cohomology-lemma-cech-vanish-basis}.
223	% As our basis $\mathcal{B}$ for the topology of $X$ we are going to use
224	% the affine opens of $X$. ... Note that the intersection of standard opens
225	% in an affine is another standard open. Hence property (1) holds. ...
226	% Finally, condition (3) of the lemma follows from
227	% Lemma \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial}."
228	\begin{proof}
229	  \uses{def:cech_complex}
230	  Write \(U = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\) and \(\mathcal{F}|_U = \widetilde{M}\) for an
231	  \(A\)-module \(M\). By Definition~\ref{def:cech_complex} the {\v C}ech complex of
232	  the standard cover is the complex of localisations
233	  \[
234	    \prod_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \;\to\;
235	    \prod_{i_0 i_1} M_{f_{i_0} f_{i_1}} \;\to\;
236	    \prod_{i_0 i_1 i_2} M_{f_{i_0} f_{i_1} f_{i_2}} \;\to\; \cdots,
237	  \]
238	  and the claim \(\check{H}^p = 0\) for \(p > 0\) is equivalent to exactness of the
239	  extended complex \(0 \to M \to \prod_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to \cdots\). Exactness may
240	  be checked after localising at an arbitrary prime \(\mathfrak{p} \subset A\).
241	  Choose an index \(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\) with
242	  \(f_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}} \notin \mathfrak{p}\); then
243	  \(M_{f_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}}, \mathfrak{p}} = M_{\mathfrak{p}}\), and the
244	  prescription \(h(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_p} = s_{i_{\mathrm{fix}} i_0 \ldots i_p}\)
245	  defines a contracting homotopy of the localised extended complex: a direct
246	  computation gives \((dh + hd)(s) = s\), so the identity is homotopic to zero and
247	  the localised complex is exact. As exactness holds after every localisation, the
248	  extended complex is exact and \(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) = 0\) for
249	  \(p > 0\).
250	
251	  For the sheaf-cohomology statement, take the affine opens of \(U\) as a basis of
252	  the topology and the standard open covers as the admissible covers. Intersections
253	  of standard opens are again standard opens, standard covers are cofinal among
254	  covers of a basic open, and the {\v C}ech vanishing just proved supplies the
255	  acyclicity hypothesis; the {\v C}ech-to-cohomology comparison on a basis then
256	  yields \(H^p(U, \mathcal{F}) = 0\) for all \(p > 0\). \emph{(This is Serre
257	  vanishing for quasi-coherent sheaves on affine schemes.)}
258	\end{proof}

260	\section{The {\v C}ech complex computes $R^i f_*$}
261	
262	\begin{lemma}
263	[{\v C}ech complex computes the higher direct images]
264	  \label{lem:cech_computes_cohomology}
265	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cech_computes_higherDirectImage}
266	  \uses{lem:cech_acyclic_affine, def:cech_complex}
267	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Tag 02KE; Lemma
268	  % \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent} ({\v C}ech computes cohomology when
269	  % all intersections are affine) and Lemma
270	  % \texttt{lemma-quasi-coherence-higher-direct-images-application}
271	  % ($H^q(X,\mathcal{F}) = H^0(S, R^q f_*\mathcal{F})$ for affine $S$)
272	  % (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L245--256 and L843--854).
273	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $X$ be a scheme.
274	  % Let $\mathcal{U} : X = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i$ be an open covering such that
275	  % $U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}$ is affine open for all $p \ge 0$ and all
276	  % $i_0, \ldots, i_p \in I$.
277	  % In this case for any quasi-coherent sheaf $\mathcal{F}$ we have
278	  % $$
279	  % \check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) = H^p(X, \mathcal{F})
280	  % $$
281	  % as $\Gamma(X, \mathcal{O}_X)$-modules for all $p$."
282	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $f : X \to S$ be a morphism of schemes.
283	  % Assume that $f$ is quasi-separated and quasi-compact.
284	  % Assume $S$ is affine.
285	  % For any quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-module $\mathcal{F}$
286	  % we have
287	  % $$
288	  % H^q(X, \mathcal{F}) = H^0(S, R^qf_*\mathcal{F})
289	  % $$
290	  % for all $q \in \mathbf{Z}$."
291	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, Tag 02KE
292	  (\texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent}) and
293	  \texttt{lemma-quasi-coherence-higher-direct-images-application}.}
294	  Let \(f : X \to S\) be a separated quasi-compact morphism of schemes, let
295	  \(\mathcal{F}\) be a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module, and let
296	  \(\mathfrak{U} : X = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) be a finite affine open cover of
297	  \(X\) (so, by separatedness, every intersection \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\) is
298	  affine). Then the cohomology sheaves of the relative {\v C}ech complex
299	  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F})\) of
300	  Definition~\ref{def:cech_complex} compute the higher direct images: for every
301	  \(i \geq 0\) there is a canonical isomorphism of \(\mathcal{O}_S\)-modules
302	  \[
303	    H^i\bigl(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F})\bigr)
304	      \;\cong\; R^i f_*\mathcal{F}.
305	  \]
306	  In particular, over an affine base \(S = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\), taking global
307	  sections gives \(H^i(X, \mathcal{F}) = \check{H}^i(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F}) =
308	  H^0(S, R^i f_*\mathcal{F})\) as \(A\)-modules for all \(i\).
309	\end{lemma}
310	
311	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "In view of
312	% Lemma \texttt{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine-cohomology-zero}
313	% this is a special case of
314	% Cohomology, Lemma
315	% \ref{cohomology-lemma-cech-spectral-sequence-application}."
316	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "Consider the Leray spectral sequence
317	% $E_2^{p, q} = H^p(S, R^qf_*\mathcal{F})$
318	% converging to $H^{p + q}(X, \mathcal{F})$, see
319	% Cohomology, Lemma \ref{cohomology-lemma-Leray}.
320	% By Lemma \ref{lemma-quasi-coherence-higher-direct-images}
321	% we see that the sheaves $R^qf_*\mathcal{F}$ are quasi-coherent.
322	% By Lemma \texttt{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine-cohomology-zero}
323	% we see that $E_2^{p, q} = 0$ when $p > 0$.
324	% Hence the spectral sequence degenerates at $E_2$ and we win."
325	\begin{proof}
326	  \uses{lem:cech_acyclic_affine, def:cech_complex}
327	  The question is local on \(S\), and the formation of both sides commutes with
328	  restriction to opens of \(S\), so we may assume \(S = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\) is
329	  affine. Because \(f\) is separated and quasi-compact, each finite intersection
330	  \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\) is affine, and \(f\) restricted to it is an affine
331	  morphism; by the affine acyclicity of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine} the
332	  higher direct images of \(\mathcal{F}\) over each such intersection vanish, so the
333	  terms of the relative {\v C}ech complex carry no hidden higher cohomology.
334	
335	  Consider the {\v C}ech-to-cohomology spectral sequence of the cover
336	  \(\mathfrak{U}\), with \(E_2\)-page
337	  \(E_2^{p,q} = \check{H}^p(\mathfrak{U}, \underline{H}^q(\mathcal{F}))\)
338	  converging to \(H^{p+q}(X, \mathcal{F})\). Since all intersections are affine,
339	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine} forces the presheaf cohomology
340	  \(\underline{H}^q(\mathcal{F})\) to vanish on the intersections for \(q > 0\), so
341	  the spectral sequence collapses to its row \(q = 0\): the {\v C}ech cohomology of
342	  \(\mathcal{F}\) for the cover \(\mathfrak{U}\) computes the sheaf cohomology,
343	  \(\check{H}^p(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F}) = H^p(X, \mathcal{F})\) for all \(p\),
344	  and the same identification holds relatively, i.e.\ the cohomology sheaves of
345	  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F})\) compute the cohomology
346	  sheaves of \(Rf_*\mathcal{F}\).
347	
348	  It remains to identify these cohomology sheaves with \(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\). Over
349	  the affine base \(S = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\), the Leray spectral sequence
350	  \(E_2^{p,q} = H^p(S, R^q f_*\mathcal{F}) \Rightarrow H^{p+q}(X, \mathcal{F})\)
351	  has \(R^q f_*\mathcal{F}\) quasi-coherent, whence \(H^p(S, R^q f_*\mathcal{F}) = 0\)
352	  for \(p > 0\) by Serre vanishing (Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine}). The Leray
353	  sequence therefore degenerates at \(E_2\), giving
354	  \(H^i(X, \mathcal{F}) = H^0(S, R^i f_*\mathcal{F})\). Combining with the previous
355	  paragraph, \(H^i\bigl(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F})\bigr)
356	  \cong R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\) as \(\mathcal{O}_S\)-modules for all \(i \geq 0\).
357	\end{proof}
358	
359	\section{The unconditional higher direct image}
360	
361	\begin{definition}
362	[Unconditional higher direct image via {\v C}ech]
363	  \label{def:cech_higher_direct_image}
364	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechHigherDirectImage}
365	  \uses{lem:cech_computes_cohomology}
366	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
367	  % \texttt{lemma-quasi-coherence-higher-direct-images-application}
368	  % (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L843--854); the unconditional
369	  % packaging via the {\v C}ech complex is an Archon-original construction grounded
370	  % in this lemma and Lemma \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent}.
371	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $f : X \to S$ be a morphism of schemes.
372	  % Assume that $f$ is quasi-separated and quasi-compact.
373	  % Assume $S$ is affine.
374	  % For any quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-module $\mathcal{F}$
375	  % we have
376	  % $$
377	  % H^q(X, \mathcal{F}) = H^0(S, R^qf_*\mathcal{F})
378	  % $$
379	  % for all $q \in \mathbf{Z}$."
380	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
381	  \texttt{lemma-quasi-coherence-higher-direct-images-application}; unconditional
382	  {\v C}ech packaging is Archon-original.}
383	  Let \(f : X \to S\) be a separated quasi-compact morphism and \(\mathcal{F}\) a
384	  quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module. Fix a finite affine open cover
385	  \(\mathfrak{U}\) of \(X\). The \emph{(unconditional) \(i\)-th higher direct
386	  image} is defined, for each \(i \geq 0\), as the \(i\)-th cohomology sheaf of the
387	  relative {\v C}ech complex,
388	  \[
389	    R^i f_*\mathcal{F} \;:=\;
390	      H^i\bigl(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F})\bigr)
391	      \in \operatorname{QCoh}(S).
392	  \]
393	  This requires \emph{no} hypothesis that the category of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules
394	  have enough injectives: the right-hand side is the cohomology of an explicit
395	  complex of quasi-coherent sheaves. By
396	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_computes_cohomology} the result is canonically isomorphic to
397	  the derived-functor higher direct image wherever the latter is defined, and in
398	  particular is independent of the chosen affine cover \(\mathfrak{U}\) up to
399	  canonical isomorphism: two affine covers admit a common affine refinement, and
400	  the induced refinement maps on {\v C}ech complexes are quasi-isomorphisms because
401	  both compute the same \(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\) by
402	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_computes_cohomology}. For \(i = 0\) one recovers the ordinary
403	  pushforward \(R^0 f_*\mathcal{F} = f_*\mathcal{F}\).
404	\end{definition}

406	\section{Flat base change for the {\v C}ech higher direct images}
407	
408	\begin{lemma}
409	[Flat base change of $R^i f_*$ via {\v C}ech]
410	  \label{lem:cech_flat_base_change}
411	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cech_flatBaseChange}
412	  \uses{def:cech_higher_direct_image, lem:cech_computes_cohomology}
413	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Tag 02KH (Lemma
414	  % \texttt{lemma-flat-base-change-cohomology}, "Flat base change")
415	  % (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L947--970).
416	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Consider a cartesian diagram of schemes
417	  % $$
418	  % \xymatrix{
419	  % X' \ar[d]_{f'} \ar[r]_{g'} & X \ar[d]^f \\
420	  % S' \ar[r]^g & S
421	  % }
422	  % $$
423	  % Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-module
424	  % with pullback $\mathcal{F}' = (g')^*\mathcal{F}$.
425	  % Assume that $g$ is flat and that $f$ is quasi-compact and quasi-separated.
426	  % For any $i \geq 0$
427	  % \begin{enumerate}
428	  % \item the base change map of
429	  % Cohomology, Lemma \ref{cohomology-lemma-base-change-map-flat-case}
430	  % is an isomorphism
431	  % $$
432	  % g^*R^if_*\mathcal{F} \longrightarrow R^if'_*\mathcal{F}',
433	  % $$
434	  % \item if $S = \Spec(A)$ and $S' = \Spec(B)$, then
435	  % $H^i(X, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B = H^i(X', \mathcal{F}')$.
436	  % \end{enumerate}"
437	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, Tag 02KH
438	  (\texttt{lemma-flat-base-change-cohomology}, ``Flat base change'').}
439	  Consider the cartesian square
440	  \[
441	    \begin{array}{ccc}
442	      X' & \xrightarrow{\;g'\;} & X \\[2pt]
443	      {\scriptstyle f'}\big\downarrow & & \big\downarrow{\scriptstyle f} \\[2pt]
444	      S' & \xrightarrow{\;g\;} & S
445	    \end{array}
446	  \]
447	  with \(f : X \to S\) separated and quasi-compact, \(\mathcal{F}\) a
448	  quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module, and
449	  \(\mathcal{F}' = (g')^*\mathcal{F}\). Assume \(g\) is flat. Then for every
450	  \(i \geq 0\) the canonical base-change map between the unconditional {\v C}ech
451	  higher direct images of Definition~\ref{def:cech_higher_direct_image} is an
452	  isomorphism
453	  \[
454	    g^*\bigl(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\bigr) \;\xrightarrow{\;\sim\;}\;
455	      R^i f'_*\bigl((g')^*\mathcal{F}\bigr) = R^i f'_*\mathcal{F}'.
456	  \]
457	  Equivalently, when \(S = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\) and
458	  \(S' = \operatorname{Spec}(B)\) with \(A \to B\) flat, the comparison map of
459	  \(B\)-modules \(H^i(X, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B \to H^i(X', \mathcal{F}')\) is an
460	  isomorphism.
461	\end{lemma}
462	
463	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "Having said this, part (1) follows from part (2). Namely,
464	% part (1) is local on $S'$ and hence we may assume $S$
465	% and $S'$ are affine. ...
466	% In case $X$ is separated, choose an affine open covering
467	% $\mathcal{U} : X = U_1 \cup \ldots \cup U_t$ and recall that
468	% $$
469	% \check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) = H^p(X, \mathcal{F}),
470	% $$
471	% see
472	% Lemma \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent}.
473	% If $\mathcal{U}_B : X_B = (U_1)_B \cup \ldots \cup (U_t)_B$ we obtain
474	% by base change, then it is still the case that each $(U_i)_B$ is affine
475	% and that $X_B$ is separated. ...
476	% We have the following relation between the {\v C}ech complexes
477	% $$
478	% \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}_B, \mathcal{F}_B) =
479	% \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B
480	% $$
481	% as follows from
482	% Lemma \ref{lemma-affine-base-change}.
483	% Since $A \to B$ is flat, the same thing remains true on taking cohomology."
484	\begin{proof}
485	  \uses{def:cech_higher_direct_image, lem:cech_computes_cohomology}
486	  The assertion that the base-change map is an isomorphism is local on \(S'\), so we
487	  may assume \(S = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\) and \(S' = \operatorname{Spec}(B)\) with
488	  \(A \to B\) flat. By Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_computes_cohomology} both
489	  \(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\) and \(R^i f'_*\mathcal{F}'\) are quasi-coherent, associated
490	  to the \(A\)-module \(H^i(X, \mathcal{F})\) and the \(B\)-module
491	  \(H^i(X', \mathcal{F}')\) respectively; since pullback along \(g\) corresponds to
492	  \(- \otimes_A B\) on modules, it suffices to prove that the comparison map
493	  \(H^i(X, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B \to H^i(X', \mathcal{F}')\) is an isomorphism.
494	
495	  Choose a finite affine open cover \(\mathfrak{U} : X = U_1 \cup \dots \cup U_t\);
496	  by separatedness all intersections \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\) are affine. Base
497	  changing the cover yields an affine open cover \(\mathfrak{U}_B\) of the
498	  again-separated \(X' = X_B\), with each \((U_{i_0 \ldots i_p})_B\) affine. By
499	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_computes_cohomology} the two {\v C}ech complexes compute the
500	  respective cohomologies. The affine base change for the \(i = 0\) direct image
501	  identifies each term of the base-changed {\v C}ech complex with the original term
502	  tensored over \(A\) with \(B\), giving an isomorphism of cochain complexes
503	  \[
504	    \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathfrak{U}_B, \mathcal{F}_B)
505	      \;\cong\;
506	      \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B.
507	  \]
508	  Because \(A \to B\) is flat, \(- \otimes_A B\) is exact and commutes with the
509	  formation of cohomology of a complex; applying \(H^i\) for any \(i \geq 0\) yields
510	  \[
511	    H^i(X, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B \;\xrightarrow{\;\sim\;}\;
512	      H^i(X', \mathcal{F}').
513	  \]
514	  Translating back to sheaves on \(S' = \operatorname{Spec}(B)\) — using that the
515	  unconditional higher direct images are the quasi-coherent sheaves associated to
516	  these modules — gives the asserted isomorphism
517	  \(g^*(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}) \xrightarrow{\sim} R^i f'_*\mathcal{F}'\) for all
518	  \(i \geq 0\). The flatness of \(g\) (hence of \(g'\), by base change) is exactly
519	  what makes \(- \otimes_A B\) exact, and is therefore the precise hypothesis under
520	  which the {\v C}ech computation of Definition~\ref{def:cech_higher_direct_image}
521	  is compatible with base change.
522	\end{proof}

6	import Mathlib

8	/-!
9	# Higher direct images `Rⁱ f_*` of quasi-coherent sheaves (`i ≥ 1`)
10	
11	This file treats the higher derived direct images `Rⁱ f_* F` for `i ≥ 1`,
12	complementing the `i = 0` (direct-image) case of `Cohomology/FlatBaseChange.lean`.
13	
14	Throughout, `f : X ⟶ S` is a morphism of schemes and `F : X.Modules` a sheaf of
15	modules on `X`. The `i`-th higher direct image `Rⁱ f_* F` is the `i`-th right
16	derived functor of the pushforward functor `f_*` applied to `F`.
17	
18	The four main declarations are:
19	
20	* `AlgebraicGeometry.higherDirectImage` — the higher direct image `Rⁱ f_* F`.
21	* `AlgebraicGeometry.higherDirectImage_isQuasiCoherent` — `Rⁱ f_* F` is
22	  quasi-coherent when `f` is quasi-compact and quasi-separated.
23	* `AlgebraicGeometry.higherDirectImage_affine_eq_zero` — `Rⁱ f_* F = 0` for
24	  `i ≥ 1` when `f` is affine.
25	* `AlgebraicGeometry.flatBaseChange_higherDirectImage_isIso` — flat base change
26	  for the higher direct images (`i ≥ 1` case).
27	
28	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_HigherDirectImage.tex`.
29	
30	Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, Tags 02KE (quasi-coherence of
31	higher direct images), 02KG (relative affine vanishing), 02KH (flat base change).
32	-/
33	
34	universe u
35	
36	open CategoryTheory Limits
37	
38	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
39	
40	open Scheme.Modules
41	
42	variable {S S' X X' : Scheme.{u}}
43	
44	/-- The `i`-th higher direct image `Rⁱ f_* F` of a sheaf of modules `F` on `X`
45	along a morphism `f : X ⟶ S`, defined as the `i`-th right derived functor of the
46	pushforward functor `f_*` applied to `F`.
47	
48	For `i = 0` this recovers the ordinary pushforward `R⁰ f_* F = f_* F`.
49	
50	Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, Definition of `Rⁱ f_*`. -/
51	noncomputable def higherDirectImage [HasInjectiveResolutions X.Modules]
52	    (f : X ⟶ S) (i : ℕ) (F : X.Modules) : S.Modules :=
53	  ((pushforward f).rightDerived i).obj F
54	
55	/-- **Quasi-coherence of higher direct images** (Stacks 02KE, part (1)).
56	If `f : X ⟶ S` is quasi-compact and quasi-separated and `F` is a quasi-coherent
57	`O_X`-module, then `Rⁱ f_* F` is a quasi-coherent `O_S`-module for every `i`.
58	
59	The proof (Stacks 02KE) is local on `S`, reducing to `S` affine; one then runs the
60	induction principle for quasi-compact opens of the quasi-compact quasi-separated
61	`X`: the affine base case uses relative affine vanishing
62	(`higherDirectImage_affine_eq_zero`) together with quasi-coherence of `f_*` for
63	the `i = 0` part, and the inductive step uses the relative Mayer–Vietoris
64	sequence, whose terms are quasi-coherent.
65	
66	Source: Stacks Project, Tag 02KE ("Quasi-coherence of higher direct images"). -/
67	theorem higherDirectImage_isQuasiCoherent [HasInjectiveResolutions X.Modules]
68	    (f : X ⟶ S) [QuasiCompact f] [QuasiSeparated f] (i : ℕ) (F : X.Modules)
69	    (hF : F.IsQuasicoherent) :
70	    (higherDirectImage f i F).IsQuasicoherent := by
71	  -- Proof (Stacks 02KE) via the induction principle for q.c. opens of the q.c.q.s.
72	  -- scheme `X`, using relative affine vanishing in the affine base case and the
73	  -- relative Mayer–Vietoris sequence in the inductive step.  Needs the explicit
74	  -- description of `Rⁱ f_*` as the sheafification of `V ↦ Hⁱ(f⁻¹ V, F|…)` and the
75	  -- Mayer–Vietoris infrastructure, both currently absent from Mathlib.
76	  sorry
77	
78	/-- **Relative affine vanishing** (Stacks 02KG).
79	If `f : X ⟶ S` is an affine morphism and `F` is a quasi-coherent `O_X`-module,
80	then `Rⁱ f_* F = 0` for all `i ≥ 1`.
81	
82	The proof (Stacks 02KG): `Rⁱ f_* F` is the sheafification of
83	`V ↦ Hⁱ(f⁻¹ V, F|_{f⁻¹ V})`; for `V` affine, `f⁻¹ V` is affine (as `f` is affine),
84	so the higher cohomology of the quasi-coherent `F|_{f⁻¹ V}` vanishes. As the affine
85	opens form a basis of `S`, the sheafification of a presheaf vanishing on a basis is
86	zero.
87	
88	Source: Stacks Project, Tag 02KG ("Relative affine vanishing"). -/
89	theorem higherDirectImage_affine_eq_zero [HasInjectiveResolutions X.Modules]
90	    (f : X ⟶ S) [IsAffineHom f] (i : ℕ) (hi : 1 ≤ i) (F : X.Modules)
91	    (hF : F.IsQuasicoherent) :
92	    IsZero (higherDirectImage f i F) := by
93	  -- Proof (Stacks 02KG): on affine opens `V ⊆ S` the preimage `f⁻¹ V` is affine,
94	  -- so `Hⁱ(f⁻¹ V, F|…) = 0` for `i ≥ 1` (vanishing of higher cohomology of a
95	  -- quasi-coherent sheaf on an affine scheme), hence the defining presheaf of
96	  -- `Rⁱ f_* F` vanishes on a basis and its sheafification is zero.  Needs the
97	  -- explicit `V ↦ Hⁱ(f⁻¹ V, F|…)` description and affine-cohomology vanishing,
98	  -- both currently absent from Mathlib for `Scheme.Modules`.
99	  sorry
100	
101	/-- **Flat base change for the higher direct images** (Stacks 02KH, `i ≥ 1` case).
102	Given a cartesian square
103	```
104	  X' --g'--> X
105	  |f'        |f
106	  v          v
107	  S' --g---> S
108	```
109	with `F` a quasi-coherent `O_X`-module, `g` flat and `f` quasi-compact and
110	quasi-separated, the canonical base-change map is an isomorphism
111	```
112	  g^*(Rⁱ f_* F) ≅ Rⁱ f'_* ((g')^* F)
113	```
114	for every `i ≥ 1`.
115	
116	The proof (Stacks 02KH) reduces to `S = Spec A`, `S' = Spec B` with `A → B` flat;
117	by quasi-coherence (`higherDirectImage_isQuasiCoherent`) the two sides correspond
118	to the `A`-module `Hⁱ(X, F)` and the `B`-module `Hⁱ(X_B, F_B)`, and one shows
119	`Hⁱ(X, F) ⊗_A B ≅ Hⁱ(X_B, F_B)` via base change of a Čech complex (separated case)
120	and the Čech-to-cohomology spectral sequence (quasi-separated case), using flatness
121	of `A → B`.
122	
123	We state the isomorphism as `Nonempty (… ≅ …)`: the canonical higher base-change
124	map (the `i ≥ 1` analogue of `pushforwardBaseChangeMap`) is not yet constructed.
125	
126	Source: Stacks Project, Tag 02KH ("Flat base change"), the `i ≥ 1` case. -/
127	theorem flatBaseChange_higherDirectImage_isIso
128	    [HasInjectiveResolutions X.Modules] [HasInjectiveResolutions X'.Modules]
129	    (f : X ⟶ S) (g : S' ⟶ S) (f' : X' ⟶ S') (g' : X' ⟶ X)
130	    (h : IsPullback g' f' f g) [Flat g] [QuasiCompact f] [QuasiSeparated f]
131	    (i : ℕ) (F : X.Modules) (hF : F.IsQuasicoherent) :
132	    Nonempty ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj (higherDirectImage f i F) ≅
133	      higherDirectImage f' i ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g').obj F)) := by
134	  -- Proof (Stacks 02KH): local on `S'`, reduce to `S = Spec A`, `S' = Spec B`,
135	  -- `A → B` flat.  By quasi-coherence the two higher direct images are the
136	  -- tilde-modules of `Hⁱ(X, F)` and `Hⁱ(X_B, F_B)`, and pullback along `g`
137	  -- corresponds to `- ⊗_A B`; the comparison `Hⁱ(X, F) ⊗_A B ≅ Hⁱ(X_B, F_B)`
138	  -- follows from base change of the Čech complex (separated case) and the
139	  -- Čech-to-cohomology spectral sequence (q.s. case), using flatness.  Needs the
140	  -- Čech-cohomology / spectral-sequence infrastructure for `Scheme.Modules`,
141	  -- currently absent from Mathlib.
142	  sorry
143	
144	end AlgebraicGeometry

8	/-!
9	# Flat base change for the pushforward of a quasi-coherent sheaf (`i = 0`)
10	
11	This file establishes the `i = 0` (direct-image) case of flat base change: the
12	formation of the pushforward `f_* F` of a quasi-coherent sheaf commutes with flat
13	base change on the target.
14	
15	Throughout we work with a (commutative, eventually cartesian) square of schemes
16	```
17	  X' --g'--> X
18	  |f'        |f
19	  v          v
20	  S' --g---> S
21	```
22	recorded by morphisms `f : X ⟶ S`, `g : S' ⟶ S`, `f' : X' ⟶ S'`, `g' : X' ⟶ X`
23	with `g' ≫ f = f' ≫ g`, and `F : X.Modules` a sheaf of modules on `X`.
24	
25	The three main declarations are:
26	
27	* `AlgebraicGeometry.pushforwardBaseChangeMap` — the canonical base-change map
28	  `g^*(f_* F) ⟶ f'_*((g')^* F)`, built as the adjoint mate of the unit of the
29	  `((g')^*, (g')_*)`-adjunction.
30	* `AlgebraicGeometry.affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso` — for `f` affine and the
31	  square cartesian, the base-change map is an isomorphism (affine case: tensor
32	  associativity).
33	* `AlgebraicGeometry.flatBaseChange_pushforward_isIso` — for `g` flat and `f`
34	  quasi-compact quasi-separated, the base-change map is an isomorphism.
35	
36	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_FlatBaseChange.tex`.
37	
38	Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, §"Cohomology and base change, I",
39	Tag 02KH.
40	-/
41	
42	universe u
43	
44	open CategoryTheory Limits
45	
46	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
47	
48	open Scheme.Modules
49	
50	variable {S S' X X' : Scheme.{u}}
51	  (f : X ⟶ S) (g : S' ⟶ S) (f' : X' ⟶ S') (g' : X' ⟶ X)
52	
53	/-- The canonical base-change map for the pushforward of a sheaf of modules.
54	
55	Given a commutative square
56	```
57	  X' --g'--> X
58	  |f'        |f
59	  v          v
60	  S' --g---> S
61	```
62	(with `comm : g' ≫ f = f' ≫ g`) and a sheaf of modules `F` on `X`, this is the
63	canonical morphism `g^*(f_* F) ⟶ f'_*((g')^* F)` of sheaves of modules on `S'`.
64	
65	It is the image, under the `(g^*, g_*)`-adjunction transpose, of the composite
66	```
67	  f_* F --f_*(unit)--> f_* (g')_* (g')^* F
68	        = (g' ≫ f)_* (g')^* F = (f' ≫ g)_* (g')^* F = g_* f'_* (g')^* F,
69	```
70	where `unit` is the unit of the `((g')^*, (g')_*)`-adjunction and the middle
71	equalities are the pseudofunctoriality of pushforward together with the
72	commutativity of the square.
73	
74	Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, §"Cohomology and base change, I",
75	base-change diagram. -/
76	noncomputable def pushforwardBaseChangeMap (comm : g' ≫ f = f' ≫ g) (F : X.Modules) :
77	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj ((pushforward f).obj F) ⟶
78	      (pushforward f').obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g').obj F) :=
79	  ((pullbackPushforwardAdjunction g).homEquiv _ _).symm
80	    ((pushforward f).map ((pullbackPushforwardAdjunction g').unit.app F) ≫

/-
Copyright (c) 2026 Christian Merten. All rights reserved.
Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
Authors: Christian Merten
-/
import Mathlib
import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.HigherDirectImage

/-!
# Čech computation of the higher direct images `Rⁱ f_*` (unconditional)

This file constructs the higher derived direct images `Rⁱ f_* F` for `i ≥ 1`
**without appealing to injective resolutions** in the category of sheaves of
modules. The companion `Cohomology/HigherDirectImage.lean` defines `Rⁱ f_*` as a
right derived functor, which requires the ambient category of `O_X`-modules to
have enough injectives — a property not currently available for sheaves of
modules over a sheaf of rings whose value category varies over the site. The
Čech approach developed here sidesteps the issue: it computes `Rⁱ f_* F` as the
cohomology of an explicit complex built from the pushforwards of `F` over the
finite intersections of an affine open cover, producing an **unconditional**
construction of `Rⁱ f_*` for quasi-coherent `F` and separated quasi-compact `f`.

Throughout, `f : X ⟶ S` is a quasi-compact, separated morphism of schemes (so
all finite intersections of an affine open cover of `X` are again affine), and
`F : X.Modules` is a quasi-coherent `O_X`-module. A base change of `f` along
`g : S' ⟶ S` is recorded by a cartesian square
```
  X' --g'--> X
  |f'        |f
  v          v
  S' --g---> S
```
with `F' = (g')^* F` the pullback of `F` to `X'`.

The six main declarations are:

* `AlgebraicGeometry.CechNerve` — the (augmented) Čech nerve of an affine open
  cover, an augmented cosimplicial object of `O_X`-modules.
* `AlgebraicGeometry.CechComplex` — the relative Čech complex in `QCoh(S)`, a
  cochain complex of `O_S`-modules whose degree-`p` term is the product of the
  pushforwards of `F` over the `(p+1)`-fold intersections of the cover.
* `AlgebraicGeometry.CechAcyclic.affine` — Čech acyclicity on affines: the Čech
  complex of a standard cover of an affine scheme has vanishing cohomology in
  all positive degrees (Serre vanishing for quasi-coherent sheaves on affines).
* `AlgebraicGeometry.cech_computes_higherDirectImage` — the cohomology of the
  relative Čech complex is canonically isomorphic to `Rⁱ f_* F` wherever the
  derived functor is defined.
* `AlgebraicGeometry.cechHigherDirectImage` — the **unconditional** `i`-th higher
  direct image, defined as the `i`-th cohomology sheaf of the relative Čech
  complex (no enough-injectives hypothesis required).
* `AlgebraicGeometry.cech_flatBaseChange` — flat base change for the
  unconditional Čech higher direct images.

See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_CechHigherDirectImage.tex`.

Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, §Čech cohomology of quasi-coherent
sheaves and §Quasi-coherence of higher direct images; Tags 02KE
(`lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent`), 02KG
(`lemma-quasi-coherent-affine-cohomology-zero`), 02KH
(`lemma-flat-base-change-cohomology`).
-/

universe u

open CategoryTheory Limits

namespace AlgebraicGeometry

open Scheme.Modules

variable {S S' X X' : Scheme.{u}}

/-- **Čech nerve of an affine open cover** (Stacks, Cohomology of Schemes, §Čech
cohomology of quasi-coherent sheaves).

For a scheme `X`, a finite affine open cover `𝒰 : X = ⋃ Uᵢ` and a quasi-coherent
sheaf `F`, the *Čech nerve* is the augmented cosimplicial object of
`O_X`-modules whose object in simplicial degree `p` is the product, over the
`(p+1)`-tuples `(i₀,…,i_p)` of indices, of the direct images
`(j_{i₀…i_p})_* (F|_{U_{i₀…i_p}})` of the restriction of `F` to the
`(p+1)`-fold intersection `U_{i₀…i_p} = U_{i₀} ∩ ⋯ ∩ U_{i_p}` along the open
immersion `j_{i₀…i_p} : U_{i₀…i_p} ↪ X`. Faces are the restriction maps that
omit one index, degeneracies repeat one index, and the augmentation in degree
`-1` is `F` itself on all of `X`. When `X` is separated each intersection
`U_{i₀…i_p}` is affine.

Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
`lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial`. -/
noncomputable def CechNerve (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (F : X.Modules) :
    CosimplicialObject.Augmented X.Modules :=
  -- Construction (Stacks): the augmented cosimplicial object
  -- `[p] ↦ ∏_{(i₀,…,i_p)} (j_{i₀…i_p})_* (F|_{U_{i₀…i_p}})` with faces the
  -- index-omitting restriction maps, degeneracies the index-repeating maps, and
  -- augmentation `F`. Requires the nerve of the cover (intersection products of
  -- pushforwards) as a functor out of `SimplexCategory`, currently absent from
  -- Mathlib for `Scheme.Modules`.
  sorry

/-- **Relative Čech complex of a quasi-coherent sheaf** (Stacks, Cohomology of
Schemes, `lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial`).

For `f : X ⟶ S`, a finite affine open cover `𝒰` of `X` (with all intersections
affine, e.g. `X` separated) and a quasi-coherent sheaf `F`, the *relative Čech
complex* `Č•(𝒰, F)` is the cochain complex of `O_S`-modules with degree-`p` term
```
  Čᵖ(𝒰, F) = ∏_{(i₀,…,i_p)} (f|_{U_{i₀…i_p}})_* (F|_{U_{i₀…i_p}}),
```
and differential the alternating sum of the restriction maps
`(d s)_{i₀…i_{p+1}} = Σⱼ (-1)ʲ s_{i₀…î_j…i_{p+1}}|_{U_{i₀…i_{p+1}}}`. Over an
affine `U = Spec A` with `F|_U = M~` and a standard cover by the `D(fᵢ)`, this is
the complex of localisations `∏ M_{f_{i₀}} → ∏ M_{f_{i₀}f_{i₁}} → ⋯`. Each term
is quasi-coherent because the intersections are affine and the pushforward of a
quasi-coherent sheaf along a quasi-compact quasi-separated morphism is
quasi-coherent.

Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
`lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial`. -/
noncomputable def CechComplex (f : X ⟶ S) (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (F : X.Modules) :
    CochainComplex S.Modules ℕ :=
  -- Construction (Stacks): the cochain complex obtained by applying the relative
  -- pushforward `f_*` over each finite intersection to the Čech nerve
  -- `CechNerve 𝒰 F`, with the alternating-sum Čech differential. Requires the
  -- nerve together with the alternating-sum face map assembled into a
  -- `CochainComplex`, currently absent from Mathlib for `Scheme.Modules`.
  sorry

/-- **Čech acyclicity on affines** (Stacks 02KG;
`lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial` and
`lemma-quasi-coherent-affine-cohomology-zero`).

Let `X = Spec A` be affine, `F` a quasi-coherent `O_X`-module, and `𝒰` a finite
standard open cover (the `fᵢ ∈ A` generate the unit ideal). Then the relative
Čech complex (here with `f` an affine morphism) has vanishing cohomology in all
positive degrees: `Hᵖ = 0` for `p ≥ 1`, equivalently `Hᵖ(X, F) = 0` for `p > 0`
(Serre vanishing for quasi-coherent sheaves on affines).

The proof (Stacks): write `F|_X = M~`; the Čech complex of the standard cover is
the complex of localisations, and `Hᵖ = 0` for `p > 0` is equivalent to exactness
of the extended complex `0 → M → ∏ M_{f_{i₀}} → ⋯`. Exactness is checked after
localising at an arbitrary prime `𝔭`; choosing `i_fix` with `f_{i_fix} ∉ 𝔭`, the
prescription `h(s)_{i₀…i_p} = s_{i_fix i₀…i_p}` is a contracting homotopy, so the
localised complex is exact, hence so is the complex. The Čech-to-cohomology
comparison on the basis of affine opens then gives the sheaf statement. -/
theorem CechAcyclic.affine [IsAffine X] (f : X ⟶ S) [IsAffineHom f]
    (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
    (F : X.Modules) (hF : F.IsQuasicoherent) (p : ℕ) (hp : 1 ≤ p) :
    IsZero ((CechComplex f 𝒰 F).homology p) := by
  -- Proof (Stacks 02KG): on the affine `X = Spec A` the Čech complex of the
  -- standard cover is the complex of localisations; positive-degree exactness
  -- follows from the prime-local contracting homotopy `h(s)_{i₀…i_p} =
  -- s_{i_fix i₀…i_p}` (where `f_{i_fix} ∉ 𝔭`), giving `(dh + hd) = id`. Needs the
  -- explicit localisation description of `CechComplex` on affines and the
  -- module-level homotopy, currently absent from Mathlib for `Scheme.Modules`.
  sorry

/-- **The Čech complex computes the higher direct images** (Stacks 02KE;
`lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent` and
`lemma-quasi-coherence-higher-direct-images-application`).

Let `f : X ⟶ S` be separated and quasi-compact, `F` a quasi-coherent
`O_X`-module, and `𝒰` a finite affine open cover of `X` (so, by separatedness,
every intersection is affine). Then the cohomology sheaves of the relative Čech
complex compute the higher direct images: for every `i ≥ 0` there is a canonical
isomorphism of `O_S`-modules
```
  Hⁱ(Č•(𝒰, F)) ≅ Rⁱ f_* F.
```
In particular, over an affine base `S = Spec A`, taking global sections gives
`Hⁱ(X, F) = Čⁱ(𝒰, F) = H⁰(S, Rⁱ f_* F)` as `A`-modules.

We state the isomorphism as `Nonempty (… ≅ …)` and compare against the
derived-functor higher direct image `higherDirectImage` wherever the latter is
defined (`HasInjectiveResolutions X.Modules`).

The proof (Stacks 02KE): the question is local on `S`, reducing to `S` affine; by
affine acyclicity (`CechAcyclic.affine`) the higher cohomology of `F` over each
affine intersection vanishes, so the Čech-to-cohomology spectral sequence
collapses to its `q = 0` row, identifying Čech cohomology with sheaf cohomology;
over affine `S` the Leray spectral sequence then degenerates (Serre vanishing for
the quasi-coherent `Rⁱ f_* F`), giving the stated isomorphism. -/
theorem cech_computes_higherDirectImage [HasInjectiveResolutions X.Modules]
    (f : X ⟶ S) [QuasiCompact f] [IsSeparated f] (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
    (F : X.Modules) (hF : F.IsQuasicoherent) (i : ℕ) :
    Nonempty ((CechComplex f 𝒰 F).homology i ≅ higherDirectImage f i F) := by
  -- Proof (Stacks 02KE): reduce to `S` affine; affine acyclicity
  -- (`CechAcyclic.affine`) collapses the Čech-to-cohomology spectral sequence to
  -- its `q = 0` row, and the Leray spectral sequence degenerates by Serre
  -- vanishing for the quasi-coherent `Rⁱ f_* F`, yielding the comparison iso.
  -- Needs the two spectral sequences for `Scheme.Modules`, currently absent from
  -- Mathlib.
  sorry

/-- **The unconditional higher direct image via Čech** (Stacks
`lemma-quasi-coherence-higher-direct-images-application`; unconditional packaging
is Archon-original).

For `f : X ⟶ S` separated quasi-compact, `F` quasi-coherent and a finite affine
open cover `𝒰` of `X`, the *(unconditional) `i`-th higher direct image* is the
`i`-th cohomology sheaf of the relative Čech complex,
```
  Rⁱ f_* F := Hⁱ(Č•(𝒰, F)) ∈ QCoh(S).
```
This requires **no** enough-injectives hypothesis: the right-hand side is the
cohomology of an explicit complex of quasi-coherent sheaves. By
`cech_computes_higherDirectImage` it agrees with the derived-functor higher
direct image wherever the latter is defined, and is independent of the chosen
affine cover up to canonical isomorphism. For `i = 0` one recovers the ordinary
pushforward `R⁰ f_* F = f_* F`. -/
noncomputable def cechHigherDirectImage (f : X ⟶ S) (𝒰 : X.OpenCover)
    (F : X.Modules) (i : ℕ) : S.Modules :=
  (CechComplex f 𝒰 F).homology i

/-- **Flat base change for the Čech higher direct images** (Stacks 02KH,
`lemma-flat-base-change-cohomology`).

Given the cartesian square
```
  X' --g'--> X
  |f'        |f
  v          v
  S' --g---> S
```
with `f` separated and quasi-compact, `F` quasi-coherent, `F' = (g')^* F`, and
`g` flat, for every `i ≥ 0` the canonical base-change map between the
unconditional Čech higher direct images is an isomorphism
```
  g^*(Rⁱ f_* F) ≅ Rⁱ f'_* ((g')^* F).
```
Equivalently, for `S = Spec A`, `S' = Spec B` with `A → B` flat, the comparison
`Hⁱ(X, F) ⊗_A B → Hⁱ(X', F')` of `B`-modules is an isomorphism.

We state the isomorphism as `Nonempty (… ≅ …)`; `𝒰` and `𝒰'` are finite affine
covers of `X` and `X' = X ×_S S'` (the latter the base change of the former).

The proof (Stacks 02KH): local on `S'`, reduce to `S = Spec A`, `S' = Spec B`,
`A → B` flat. Base changing the cover, the affine base change for the `i = 0`
direct image identifies each term of the base-changed Čech complex with the
original tensored over `A` with `B`, giving `Č•(𝒰_B, F_B) ≅ Č•(𝒰, F) ⊗_A B`;
flatness of `A → B` makes `- ⊗_A B` exact, so it commutes with `Hⁱ`, yielding the
isomorphism. -/
theorem cech_flatBaseChange
    (f : X ⟶ S) (g : S' ⟶ S) (f' : X' ⟶ S') (g' : X' ⟶ X)
    (h : IsPullback g' f' f g) [Flat g] [QuasiCompact f] [IsSeparated f]
    (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] (𝒰' : X'.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰'.I₀]
    (F : X.Modules) (hF : F.IsQuasicoherent) (i : ℕ) :
    Nonempty ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj (cechHigherDirectImage f 𝒰 F i) ≅
      cechHigherDirectImage f' 𝒰' ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g').obj F) i) := by
  -- Proof (Stacks 02KH): local on `S'`, reduce to `S = Spec A`, `S' = Spec B`,
  -- `A → B` flat; base change of the cover and the affine `i = 0` base change give
  -- `Č•(𝒰_B, F_B) ≅ Č•(𝒰, F) ⊗_A B`, and flatness makes `- ⊗_A B` commute with
  -- `Hⁱ`. Needs the term-wise affine base change of the Čech complex and exactness
  -- of `- ⊗_A B` on `Scheme.Modules`, currently absent from Mathlib.
  sorry

end AlgebraicGeometry

| `lem:cech_computes_cohomology` | `AlgebraicGeometry.cech_computes_higherDirectImage` | `theorem : Nonempty (…homology i ≅ higherDirectImage f i F)` | typed `sorry` |
| `def:cech_higher_direct_image` | `AlgebraicGeometry.cechHigherDirectImage` | `def := (CechComplex f 𝒰 F).homology i` | **REAL BODY (no sorry)** |
| `lem:cech_flat_base_change` | `AlgebraicGeometry.cech_flatBaseChange` | `theorem : Nonempty (g^*(cech…) ≅ cech…')` | typed `sorry` |

## Design decisions (for the next prover + reviewer)

- **Imports:** `import Mathlib` + `import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.HigherDirectImage`
  (to compare against the project's derived-functor `higherDirectImage` in
  `cech_computes_higherDirectImage`). HigherDirectImage imports only Mathlib, so
  the dependency is acyclic and race-free. Did NOT import any Picard/substrate
  file — fully independent of the group-law lanes (strategy-critic sc261 #2).
- **Cover input:** the current Mathlib `Scheme.OpenCover` is
  `(Scheme.precoverage IsOpenImmersion).ZeroHypercover X`. Field names changed:
  index type is `𝒰.I₀`, the `j`-th scheme is `𝒰.X j`, the map is `𝒰.f j`,
  finiteness is `[Finite 𝒰.I₀]`. (The old `𝒰.J` / `𝒰.obj` / `𝒰.map` are GONE —
  noted for whoever fills the bodies.)
- **`Scheme.Modules` is `Abelian`** (`Scheme.Modules.instAbelian`, noncomputable),
  and `CochainComplex S.Modules ℕ` has `.homology i : S.Modules`. This is the
  spine of the construction: `cechHigherDirectImage := (CechComplex …).homology i`
  is a genuine definition (only `CechComplex`'s body is deferred).
- **Separated morphism class** is `[IsSeparated f]` (used in
  `cech_computes_higherDirectImage` and `cech_flatBaseChange`); affine morphism is
  `[IsAffineHom f]`; `[IsAffine X]`, `[QuasiCompact f]`, `[Flat g]`,
  `IsPullback g' f' f g`, `F.IsQuasicoherent` all as in HigherDirectImage.lean.
- **`Nonempty (… ≅ …)` packaging** for the two comparison theorems
  (`cech_computes_higherDirectImage`, `cech_flatBaseChange`) mirrors
  `flatBaseChange_higherDirectImage_isIso` in HigherDirectImage.lean: the
  canonical comparison maps are not yet constructed, so existence of the iso is
  the honest statement.
- **`CechAcyclic.affine`** is phrased on the relative `CechComplex` with
  `[IsAffine X] [IsAffineHom f]` (relative affine vanishing reflected in the Čech
  complex), faithful to `lem:cech_acyclic_affine` ("Čech acyclicity on affines",
  Serre vanishing).

my write domain.

## Next steps (prover lane now OPEN)

The engine lane can now be dispatched as a real prover task. Genuine-content
order (bottom-up):
1. **`CechNerve` + `CechComplex`** — the hard core. Needs: the nerve of the
   cover as a functor out of `SimplexCategory` (products over intersections of
   pushforwards `(j_{i₀…i_p})_* (F|…)`), then the alternating-sum differential
   assembled into a `CochainComplex S.Modules ℕ`. No Mathlib `Scheme.Modules`
   Čech machinery exists — this is a ground-up build (likely its own helper
   section). Consider the standard-cover affine model first (`∏ M_{f_{i₀}} → ⋯`).
2. **`CechAcyclic.affine`** — the prime-local contracting homotopy
   `h(s)_{i₀…i_p} = s_{i_fix i₀…i_p}` once `CechComplex` has the explicit
   localisation description on affines.
3. **`cech_computes_higherDirectImage`** — Čech-to-cohomology + Leray spectral
   sequences (both absent from Mathlib for `Scheme.Modules`); this is the
   heaviest and may need a spectral-sequence sub-lane.
4. **`cech_flatBaseChange`** — term-wise affine base change of the Čech complex
   (reuse `affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso` from FlatBaseChange.lean) + exactness
   of `- ⊗_A B` under flatness commuting with `Hⁱ`.
